_meta.note: Curated subset of D3FEND 1.0 techniques mapped to the offensive coverage in atlas-ttps.json and cve-catalog.json. Per AGENTS.md rule #10, only D3FEND IDs verified against the canonical ontology are included; uncertain IDs were omitted rather than fabricated. Per rule #9, every entry declares ai_pipeline_applicability — defenses architecturally impossible in ephemeral/serverless/AI pipeline contexts state an explicit alternative. Per rule #2 (framework lag), lag_notes capture the delta between framework-prescribed control behavior and the D3FEND-defined defensive behavior.

source_confidence.note: B = usually reliable; 2 = probably true. Per-entry overrides via entry-level source_confidence field. Public-record catalogs (NVD, ATLAS, CWE, RFC, framework publishers) get A1 (completely reliable, confirmed). Project-curated catalogs (zeroday-lessons, exploit-availability) default to B2 with source citations.

freshness_policy.note: Per-entry last_verified governs decay. Skills depending on this catalog must check entry freshness before high-stakes use.

_meta.bulk_intake_note: v0.13.18: +110 D3FEND techniques imported from the MITRE D3FEND OWL ontology (d3fend.mitre.org/ontologies/d3fend.json). Auto-imported entries flagged _auto_imported:true + _intake_method:v0.13.18-bulk-mitre-d3fend-owl for downstream curation.

D3-EAL.name: Executable Allowlisting
D3-EAL.description: Using a digital signature or other artifact-identifying property to restrict process execution to a pre-approved set of executables, blocking arbitrary binaries from running regardless of file path or user privilege.
D3-EAL.ai_pipeline_applicability: Partially applicable. In serverless and ephemeral container runtimes the OS-level allowlister cannot be installed; equivalent control is achieved by signed-image policy at the registry admission controller (Sigstore/cosign verification, Kyverno/OPA Gatekeeper image-signature rules) plus immutable rootfs. For MCP server runtimes the equivalent is binary-hash pinning per D3-EHB.
D3-EAL.lag_notes: NIST CM-7(1) prescribes 'least functionality' and CM-7(2) 'authorized software' but does not require cryptographic verification; many auditors accept inventory-only implementations. D3-EAL requires the runtime to actively block non-listed binaries — a behavior gap that paper-compliant CM-7 implementations routinely fail.
D3-EAL.description_full: Using a digital signature to authenticate a file before opening.

D3-EHB.name: Executable Hashbased Allowlist
D3-EHB.description: Restricting execution to binaries whose cryptographic hash matches a pre-approved allowlist entry, providing stronger identity guarantees than path- or signer-based allowlisting because hashes change on any binary modification.
implementation_examples: MCP server binary hash pinning in client config
in-toto / SLSA Level 3 attestation for ML model and tool binaries
D3-EHB.ai_pipeline_applicability: Highly applicable to AI pipelines — directly addresses HuggingFace typosquat, malicious model weight files, and MCP server supply chain compromise (CVE-2026-30615 class). For ephemeral inference containers the hash check moves from runtime to admission control (registry signature plus model weight SHA-256 verification at load time).
D3-EHB.lag_notes: SA-12 supply-chain controls do not specify cryptographic hash verification at load time, and no framework yet requires hash pinning of pretrained model weights or MCP server binaries. Auditors accept SBOM presence as SA-12 evidence; D3-EHB requires the runtime to actively reject unpinned binaries, which most SBOM programs do not implement.
D3-EHB.description_full: Monitoring the security status of an endpoint by sending periodic messages with health status, where absence of a response may indicate that the endpoint has been compromised.

D3-PSEP.name: Process Segment Execution Prevention
D3-PSEP.description: Marking memory regions as non-executable so injected shellcode or data written to data segments cannot be executed by the CPU. Implemented as DEP / NX bit on x86_64 and PXN/UXN on ARM.
D3-PSEP.ai_pipeline_applicability: Inherited from the underlying kernel; container and serverless workloads receive DEP/NX automatically. Not a workload-configurable control in ephemeral environments — verify only that the host kernel has SMEP/SMAP enabled and that JIT runtimes (Python ML stacks with native extensions) do not disable W^X for performance.
D3-PSEP.lag_notes: SI-16 (memory protection) is present in nearly all compliance baselines but never specifies SMEP/SMAP/PXN enforcement at the kernel level. For Copy Fail class LPEs (CVE-2026-49105) DEP is a prerequisite, not a sufficient defense — the exploit operates inside the kernel's executable mapping where SMEP/SMAP applies but is bypassable via the underlying class flaw.
D3-PSEP.description_full: Preventing execution of any address in a memory region other than the code segment.

D3-ASLR.name: Address Space Layout Randomization
D3-ASLR.description: Randomizing the base addresses of executable, stack, heap, and library memory regions to prevent attackers from reliably hard-coding addresses in exploit payloads.
implementation_examples: PIE binaries with full ASLR (Linux userland)
D3-ASLR.ai_pipeline_applicability: Inherited at host kernel level; not workload-configurable in containers or serverless. For confidential-compute AI pipelines (TEE-enclosed inference), ASLR is provided by the TEE runtime but with smaller entropy budgets — treat as defense-in-depth, not primary control.
D3-ASLR.lag_notes: ASLR is universally assumed by SI-16 evidence but framework controls never specify the entropy budget required. Modern info-leak primitives (KASLR-defeating side channels, deterministic-allocator LPEs like Copy Fail) trivially bypass standard KASLR; lag is in framework's failure to require FG-KASLR or equivalent.

D3-SCP.name: System Call Filtering
D3-SCP.description: Restricting the set of system calls a process can invoke, reducing the kernel attack surface available from a compromised userland process. Implemented as seccomp-bpf on Linux, syscall filters on Windows.
implementation_examples: Windows Process Mitigation Policy syscall filters
Kubernetes RuntimeDefault and Localhost seccomp profiles
D3-SCP.ai_pipeline_applicability: Directly applicable and recommended for AI inference workloads. Default seccomp profile blocks the syscall surface most kernel-LPE PoCs depend on (e.g., io_uring entry points for Copy Fail class). MCP server processes should run under a RuntimeDefault profile at minimum; tighter Localhost profiles recommended for code-execution MCP tools.
D3-SCP.lag_notes: No major framework requires seccomp profiles or equivalent syscall filtering. SC-39 (process isolation) is satisfied by namespaces alone in most audits, leaving the kernel surface fully exposed. Compliance theater: 'we use containers' is accepted as SC-39 evidence even when containers run with --privileged or no seccomp profile.
D3-SCP.description_full: Restricting system configuration modifications to a specific user or group of users.

D3-PHRA.name: Process Hardware Resource Access
D3-PHRA.description: Controlling and restricting the hardware resources (CPU features, devices, IOMMU mappings, performance counters) that a process can access, limiting both side-channel attacks and direct hardware abuse paths.
implementation_examples: GPU access scoping for ML workloads (NVIDIA MIG, MPS isolation)
D3-PHRA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Critical for multi-tenant GPU inference platforms — without MIG/MPS isolation a tenant can side-channel adjacent tenants' models. Standard container runtimes do not enforce GPU isolation by default. For confidential AI pipelines, IOMMU enforcement on accelerator passthrough is the equivalent control.
D3-PHRA.lag_notes: No framework prescribes GPU or accelerator isolation requirements. SC-39 is interpreted as CPU-process isolation; the AI accelerator side-channel surface is invisible to compliance audits. NIST AI RMF MEASURE-2.7 mentions infrastructure security but does not require hardware-level tenant isolation.

D3-MENCR.name: Message Encryption
D3-MENCR.description: Encrypting messages in transit between two parties to preserve confidentiality and, when authenticated encryption is used, integrity. Includes transport-layer (TLS) and application-layer (S/MIME, signed-and-encrypted JWT, MLS) approaches.
implementation_examples: TLS 1.3 with hybrid X25519MLKEM768 key exchange (RFC 9794-class drafts)
Signed and encrypted JWTs for service-to-service auth
D3-MENCR.ai_pipeline_applicability: Universally applicable; AI API traffic should use TLS 1.3 with PQC-hybrid key exchange per CNSA 2.0 timelines. Note: encrypted AI API traffic remains a covert C2 channel (AML.T0096) — D3-MENCR protects confidentiality but does not address content-layer abuse; pair with D3-CSPP and D3-NTA.
D3-MENCR.lag_notes: SC-8 / SC-13 require 'approved cryptography' but neither NIST 800-53 Rev 5 nor ISO 27001:2022 yet mandates PQC migration. CNSA 2.0 sets a 2030-2033 NSS deadline; commercial frameworks have not adopted equivalent timelines. Hybrid KEM deployment (ML-KEM + X25519) is current best practice and exceeds compliance requirements at every major framework.
D3-MENCR.description_full: Encrypting a message body using a cryptographic key.

D3-FE.name: File Encryption
D3-FE.description: Encrypting files at rest so their contents cannot be read without the decryption key, protecting against offline attacks on stolen storage media or unauthorized filesystem access.
implementation_examples: envelope encryption with KMS-managed DEKs (AWS KMS, GCP KMS)
D3-FE.ai_pipeline_applicability: Applies to model weight files, training datasets, and embedding stores at rest. For ephemeral inference workloads the practical control shifts to encrypted-at-rest object storage (S3 SSE-KMS) with the AI pipeline never persisting state locally. Note: harvest-now-decrypt-later means data encrypted today under RSA-2048 wrapping is exposed to post-2030 quantum decryption — pair with PQC roadmap.
D3-FE.lag_notes: SC-28 is universally implemented but rarely specifies algorithm strength or post-quantum readiness. Compliance audits accept AES-256 at-rest as sufficient; D3-FE has no opinion on long-tail confidentiality requirements where HNDL adversaries are active.
D3-FE.description_full: Encrypting a file using a cryptographic key.

D3-CBAN.name: Certificate-based Authentication
D3-CBAN.description: Authenticating a party using a digital certificate that binds an identity to a public key, where possession of the corresponding private key proves identity. Stronger than password authentication because secrets are not transmitted.
implementation_examples: client certificates for MCP server authentication
D3-CBAN.ai_pipeline_applicability: Recommended for service-to-service AI infrastructure (model registry, inference gateway, MCP server-client). Workload identity via SPIFFE is the standard pattern for ephemeral AI workloads where long-lived secrets are not viable.
D3-CBAN.lag_notes: IA-2 requires multi-factor but accepts SMS / TOTP as 'something you have' — both phishable. D3-CBAN with hardware-bound private keys (FIDO2, TPM-resident mTLS) is materially stronger but not required by any framework below AAL3 / PCI-MFA-CDE-only contexts. Certificate algorithm agility for PQC migration (ML-DSA, SLH-DSA) is not yet a compliance requirement.
D3-CBAN.description_full: Requiring a digital certificate in order to authenticate a user.

D3-MFA.name: Multi-factor Authentication
D3-MFA.description: Requiring two or more distinct authentication factors (knowledge, possession, inherence) to verify identity, raising the cost of credential-based intrusion.
D3-MFA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Applies to human access to AI control planes (model registry, fine-tuning consoles, MCP client configuration). Does not apply to machine-to-machine AI calls — use D3-CBAN there. Phishable MFA (SMS, basic push) is bypassed by AI-assisted phishing at industrial scale; FIDO2 is the only durable form.
D3-MFA.lag_notes: Every framework requires MFA but most accept any second factor as sufficient. CISA, NIST 800-63B-rev4, and OMB M-22-09 require phishing-resistant MFA for federal systems; most commercial frameworks (PCI, SOC 2) still accept SMS. The lag is in factor quality, not factor count.
D3-MFA.description_full: Requiring proof of two or more pieces of evidence in order to authenticate a user.

D3-CA.name: Certificate Analysis
D3-CA.description: Analyzing certificates observed in network traffic or installed on systems for indicators of malicious use: typosquatted CNs, anomalous issuers, short-lived certs to suspicious domains, or unexpected SANs.
implementation_examples: Certificate Transparency log monitoring (Sigsum, CT Watch)
MCP server TLS certificate pinning and rotation audit
D3-CA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Applicable to outbound AI API traffic and MCP server endpoints. For AI-API C2 detection (AML.T0096), certificate analysis alone is insufficient because legitimate AI provider certificates are used — pair with D3-CSPP and D3-NTA.
D3-CA.lag_notes: SI-4 / SC-17 do not require Certificate Transparency monitoring or JA4 fingerprinting. Most SOCs do not enrich TLS connections with certificate metadata at all; lag is in operational implementation, not control text.
D3-CA.description_full: Analyzing Public Key Infrastructure certificates to detect if they have been misconfigured or spoofed using both network traffic, certificate fields and third-party logs.

D3-DA.name: Domain Analysis
D3-DA.description: Analyzing domain names observed in traffic or configurations for indicators of malicious activity: newly registered domains, DGA patterns, homoglyph or typosquat similarity to known-good domains, lookalike domains for AI providers.
implementation_examples: Lookalike-domain monitoring for AI provider hostnames (api.openai.com, claude.ai)
D3-DA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Highly applicable to MCP supply chain attacks where typosquatted package or domain names install rogue MCP servers (CVE-2026-30615 vector). For AI-API C2, less effective because attackers use legitimate AI provider domains — domain analysis must combine with traffic content analysis (D3-CSPP).
D3-DA.lag_notes: Compliance frameworks do not specify NOD blocking or DGA detection. SI-4(4) requires 'inbound and outbound communications traffic monitoring' but does not prescribe domain reputation enrichment. The lookalike-AI-domain detection class is novel and absent from all standard control sets.
D3-DA.description_full: Executing or opening a file in a synthetic "sandbox" environment to determine if the file is a malicious program or if the file exploits another program such as a document reader.

D3-NTA.name: Network Traffic Analysis
D3-NTA.description: Analyzing network traffic communication characteristics — volumes, timing, periodicity, packet sizes, entropy — to identify malicious activity that may be invisible to content inspection (e.g., encrypted C2 channels).
implementation_examples: Encrypted traffic analysis via metadata (Cisco ETA, JA4)
Statistical entropy scoring on prompt payloads
D3-NTA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Primary control for AML.T0096 (AI-API C2). Because the AI provider endpoint is allowlisted by SC-7 in nearly all enterprises, the entire C2 lifecycle is inside the trust boundary; NTA on AI-API flows is the only practical detection. Requires per-process attribution — flow-only analysis at the network boundary cannot distinguish legitimate from malicious AI use.
D3-NTA.lag_notes: No framework requires NTA on AI-API traffic specifically. SI-4 is interpreted as IDS at the perimeter; AI-API egress is categorically allowed and unmonitored. SesameOp and PROMPTFLUX operate entirely within compliance-accepted egress patterns.
D3-NTA.description_full: Analyzing intercepted or summarized computer network traffic to detect unauthorized activity.

D3-NTPM.name: Network Traffic Policy Mapping
D3-NTPM.description: Establishing and maintaining a baseline of permitted network communication patterns — who talks to whom, on which ports, with what protocols — so deviations can be detected by other techniques.
implementation_examples: Per-process AI API egress allowlist (only build agents may reach api.anthropic.com)
D3-NTPM.ai_pipeline_applicability: Critical for AI-pipeline security. Without per-process AI-API egress policy, AML.T0096 detection is structurally impossible — every workload becomes a potential C2 endpoint. In serverless contexts, NTPM is enforced at the egress VPC endpoint or service mesh sidecar layer.
D3-NTPM.lag_notes: SC-7 is implemented as boundary firewall in almost all environments; per-process or per-workload egress allowlists are rare. AC-4 (information flow enforcement) is satisfied by network segmentation evidence even when intra-zone egress is unrestricted — exactly the lag AI-API C2 exploits.
D3-NTPM.description_full: Network traffic policy mapping identifies and models the allowed pathways of data at the network, transport, and/or application levels.

D3-CSPP.name: Client-server Payload Profiling
D3-CSPP.description: Analyzing the payloads exchanged between clients and servers for indicators of malicious use — anomalous sizes, encodings, command-like structures, or content that diverges from expected application semantics.
implementation_examples: AI-API request body inspection at egress proxy (CloudFlare AI Gateway, LiteLLM proxy)
Steganographic-encoding entropy scoring on prompt content
MCP tool-call argument schema validation and anomaly scoring
D3-CSPP.ai_pipeline_applicability: Most directly applicable defense against AML.T0096. Requires terminating-proxy architecture for AI API traffic (egress gateway with TLS interception or sanctioned SDK that logs structured payloads). Architecturally impossible when applications call AI APIs directly with org-managed TLS to the provider — alternative is mandated SDK with logging hooks.
D3-CSPP.lag_notes: SI-4(11) (analyze communications traffic anomalies) was written for traditional protocols; no framework guidance exists for AI-prompt content inspection. The prompt-as-payload analysis class did not exist when control catalogs were last revised. Compliance-driven SOCs are not equipped to ingest or analyze prompt traffic.
D3-CSPP.description_full: Comparing client-server request and response payloads to a baseline profile to identify outliers.

D3-IOPR.name: Input/Output Profiling Resource
D3-IOPR.description: Profiling the input and output behavior of a process or resource to establish a baseline and detect anomalies, including unexpected I/O patterns, unusual data flows, or access to unexpected resources.
implementation_examples: LLM output classifier for safety-bypass content (Llama Guard, Granite Guardian)
Semantic divergence scoring (user intent vs. AI action)
AI API query rate / similarity clustering for probe detection
D3-IOPR.ai_pipeline_applicability: Primary detection control for prompt injection (AML.T0051) and model ontology discovery (AML.T0017). Applies natively to AI pipelines — operates at the application layer where ephemeral runtime is irrelevant. Output-side IOPR (classifying AI responses) is more reliable than input-side (prompt classification) given the variety of injection encodings.
D3-IOPR.lag_notes: NIST AI RMF MEASURE-2.5 recommends 'measurement of AI system trustworthiness characteristics' but is non-prescriptive. No framework requires runtime output safety scoring or input adversarial classification. OWASP LLM Top 10 documents the need; compliance frameworks have not incorporated it.
D3-IOPR.description_full: Limiting access to computer input/output (IO) ports to restrict unauthorized devices.

D3-RPA.name: Remote Process Analysis
D3-RPA.description: Analyzing processes running on remote hosts via agent-based or agentless telemetry to detect malicious behaviors — unexpected child processes, anomalous network behavior by identity, or process-resource correlations indicating compromise.
implementation_examples: EDR with process-network correlation (CrowdStrike, SentinelOne, Defender for Endpoint)
Sysmon + Sigma rules for AI-API egress by unexpected process
Process identity attribution on AI API outbound flows
D3-RPA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Applies to traditional workloads. Serverless and FaaS contexts have no persistent process to analyze — alternative is invocation-level telemetry from the platform (CloudWatch Lambda Insights, GCP Cloud Run audit logs) correlated with AI-API egress flow records.
D3-RPA.lag_notes: Process-to-AI-API correlation is not part of any standard EDR ruleset. SI-4(2) (automated tools and mechanisms) requires SIEM/EDR presence but not the specific correlation rule. Most SOCs lack the process-identity attribution needed to distinguish legitimate from malicious AI-API use at the host.
D3-RPA.description_full: The detection of an internal host relaying traffic between the internal network and the external network.

D3-FAPA.name: File Access Pattern Analysis
D3-FAPA.description: Analyzing patterns of file access — which files, by which processes, in which sequence — to detect anomalies suggesting credential theft, staging, or unauthorized data access.
implementation_examples: model weight file access monitoring (who reads .safetensors at unusual hours)
D3-FAPA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Applies to persistent inference and training infrastructure. For serverless inference the alternative is object-storage access logging (S3 server access logs, GCS audit logs) with anomaly analysis on model-weight object reads.
D3-FAPA.lag_notes: AU-2 / AU-6 require auditing and review but do not require behavioral analytics on file access. UEBA on file access is a SIEM-vendor feature, not a compliance requirement. Lag is between control text ('audit appropriate events') and operational behavior detection.
D3-FAPA.description_full: Analyzing the files accessed by a process to identify unauthorized activity.

D3-NI.name: Network Isolation
D3-NI.description: Restricting which hosts and networks a system can communicate with, reducing both inbound attack surface and outbound exfiltration / C2 opportunity.
implementation_examples: VPC egress allowlists with no default internet route
AI API egress proxy as sole route to provider endpoints
D3-NI.ai_pipeline_applicability: Highly applicable: AI workloads should route to AI providers only through a sanctioned egress proxy that enforces per-workload identity, logs payloads (D3-CSPP), and applies traffic policy (D3-NTPM). Default-deny egress is the only structural defense against AML.T0096 — direct AI-API egress from arbitrary workloads makes C2 detection unreliable.
D3-NI.lag_notes: SC-7 boundary-protection language is satisfied by perimeter firewalls; default-deny egress at workload granularity is not required by any framework. AC-4 information-flow enforcement is satisfied by network zoning; per-process or per-pod egress policy is rare in audited environments.
D3-NI.description_full: Network Isolation techniques prevent network hosts from accessing non-essential system network resources.

D3-PA.name: Process Analysis
D3-PA.description: Analyzing the properties and behavior of processes (parent-child relationships, command-line arguments, loaded modules, system call patterns) to identify malicious activity.
implementation_examples: Sysmon process-creation events with parent-child Sigma rules
Linux audit / ausearch on execve with command-line capture
MCP server process-tree anomaly detection (unexpected spawn of shell from MCP host)
D3-PA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Applies to persistent hosts running MCP servers, AI coding assistants, or AI build agents. For serverless invocations, equivalent is per-invocation runtime telemetry (Lambda extension hooks, Cloud Run service workload identity logs) correlated with the AI-API call graph.
D3-PA.lag_notes: SI-4 / AU-2 require monitoring but do not require parent-child process-tree analytics. Most enterprise audits accept 'we have EDR' as evidence regardless of whether behavioral rules are tuned. Lag is in operationalization — control existence vs. detection quality.
D3-PA.description_full: Process Analysis consists of observing a running application process and analyzing it to watch for certain behaviors or conditions which may indicate adversary activity. Analysis can occur inside of the process or through a third-party monitoring application. Examples include monitoring system and privileged calls, monitoring process initiation chains, and memory boundary allocations.

D3-ANCI.name: Authentication Cache Invalidation
D3-ANCI.description: Forcibly invalidating cached or active authentication artifacts (tokens, session IDs, cached Kerberos tickets, browser SSO cookies) so that a compromised credential cannot be reused after detection. Distinct from credential rotation in that it acts on the live session state, not just the stored material.
implementation_examples: OAuth refresh-token revocation on detected credential compromise
klist purge + ticket-granting-service revocation on suspicious Kerberos use
Browser-side single-sign-on cookie invalidation pushed via IDP signal
Service-account JWT issuer kid-rotation followed by global verifier refresh
D3-ANCI.ai_pipeline_applicability: Applies to AI assistant identities (MCP server tokens, model-provider API keys). For ephemeral AI agents the cache invalidation is per-invocation; for long-lived AI sessions (assistant subscriptions), provider must expose a revoke API the operator can call.
D3-ANCI.lag_notes: AC-12 (session termination) speaks to user sessions; service-to-service token invalidation under credential compromise is rarely audited. Operationalization gap: most orgs lack the integration to actively invalidate on detection.
D3-ANCI.description_full: Removing tokens or credentials from an authentication cache to prevent further user associated account accesses.

D3-CAA.name: Credential Access Auditing
D3-CAA.description: Recording and analyzing every access to credential stores (cloud secret managers, password vaults, KMS, environment-variable reads on privileged processes) to detect anomalous read patterns indicating credential theft or misuse.
implementation_examples: AWS Secrets Manager + CloudTrail GetSecretValue audit with anomaly baseline per principal
HashiCorp Vault audit log forwarded to SIEM with per-policy read-rate alerting
Linux audit on /proc/<pid>/environ reads outside the owning process
GitHub Actions secret-access audit + repository-event correlation
D3-CAA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Applies wherever AI agents fetch credentials at runtime (MCP server bootstrap, fine-tuning job startup). Serverless equivalent: cloud-provider native secret-fetch audit (Secrets Manager VPC endpoint logs, GCP Secret Manager IAM audit logs).
D3-CAA.lag_notes: AU-2 prescribes audit event categories; framework controls do not require per-secret access baselining. Compliance audits accept 'logging is enabled' without requiring detection rules on read anomalies.
D3-CAA.description_full: Analyzing failed connections in a network to detect unauthorized activity.

D3-CH.name: Credential Hardening
D3-CH.description: Increasing the cryptographic and operational strength of credentials at rest and in transit — memory-hard password hashing, hardware-backed key storage, short credential lifetime, mandatory MFA on high-impact identities.
implementation_examples: Argon2id password hashing with tuned m/t/p
TPM/HSM-backed private keys (Windows Hello for Business, Apple Secure Enclave, AWS CloudHSM)
Short-lived OIDC tokens with mandatory refresh-token rotation
D3-CH.ai_pipeline_applicability: Applies to credentials issued to AI agents — model-provider keys should be short-lived and rotated; MCP server tokens should use mTLS or signed JWTs rather than long-lived bearer secrets.
D3-CH.lag_notes: IA-5 covers authenticator strength categorically; framework audit rarely samples the actual KDF in use. SP 800-63B's 2022 iteration-count update (PBKDF2 ≥ 600,000) lags in many compliance attestations citing the 2017 numbers.
D3-CH.description_full: Credential Hardening techniques modify system or network properties in order to protect system or network/domain credentials.

D3-EI.name: Execution Isolation
D3-EI.description: Constraining a process so that even successful exploitation cannot reach resources outside the isolation boundary — containers with read-only rootfs, sandboxed renderers, seccomp-restricted syscall sets, namespace-isolated workers, gVisor / Firecracker microVMs.
implementation_examples: Read-only container rootfs + tmpfs for ephemeral state
seccomp-bpf default-deny syscall profiles per workload
Firecracker microVMs for multi-tenant SaaS execution
Linux user namespaces dropping CAP_SYS_ADMIN at process start
D3-EI.ai_pipeline_applicability: Critical for AI agent execution: untrusted-code-execution tools (interpreter, code-runner MCP servers) must run in a microVM or gVisor sandbox, not in the host AI's process. Serverless platforms provide this implicitly (Lambda firecracker, Cloud Run gVisor).
D3-EI.lag_notes: SC-39 covers process isolation conceptually; framework controls do not specify required isolation primitives. 'Containers' alone do not satisfy isolation without read-only rootfs + seccomp + capability dropping — controls rarely audit which container hardening is actually enforced.
D3-EI.description_full: Execution Isolation techniques prevent application processes from accessing non-essential system resources, such as memory, devices, or files.

D3-FCR.name: File Content Rules
D3-FCR.description: Inspecting file contents against rule sets (YARA, Sigma, custom regex, ML classifier) to detect malicious patterns, embedded secrets, or unauthorized content classes at rest or at egress.
implementation_examples: gitleaks / trufflehog pre-commit and CI-time secret scanning
YARA rules on uploaded files at SaaS file-upload boundaries
DLP content-classification on outbound email + cloud-storage uploads
AI prompt-content classification before egress to public LLM endpoints
D3-FCR.ai_pipeline_applicability: Critical for AI exfil prevention: prompt content rules block sending sensitive data to public AI endpoints; retrieval-corpus content rules block injection of attacker-controlled documents. For RAG: per-document content classification at ingest.
D3-FCR.lag_notes: SI-3 / SI-4 cover monitoring categorically; rule-set freshness and tuning are operational concerns rarely audited. DLP frameworks lag in covering AI prompts as an egress channel.
D3-FCR.description_full: Employing a pattern matching rule language to analyze the content of files.

D3-KBPI.name: Kernel-Based Process Isolation
D3-KBPI.description: Using kernel primitives (namespaces, cgroups, seccomp, capabilities, LSMs, eBPF) to enforce isolation boundaries between processes that share the same kernel. Distinct from D3-EI in that the isolation is enforced inside a shared kernel rather than across a hypervisor or microVM boundary.
implementation_examples: SELinux / AppArmor confinement profiles per workload
Linux user namespaces + capability dropping (CAP_SYS_ADMIN removed)
eBPF LSM hooks for fine-grained policy enforcement
D3-KBPI.ai_pipeline_applicability: Less applicable on managed serverless (no kernel-tuning surface). Critical on self-managed hosts running MCP servers, AI build agents, training pipelines. Containerized AI runtimes still rely on kernel isolation primitives — a kernel LPE escapes the container.
D3-KBPI.lag_notes: SC-39 process isolation is named but not parameterized; framework controls accept 'containers are used' as evidence without auditing the kernel-level confinement layer. KASLR + SMEP + SMAP + KPTI presence is implicit not explicit in any framework control.
D3-KBPI.description_full: Using kernel-level capabilities to isolate processes.

D3-SCA.name: System Call Analysis
D3-SCA.description: Recording and analyzing system calls made by processes to detect malicious behavior — unusual syscall patterns, attempts to disable security mechanisms, kernel exploitation primitives (e.g. unshare(2), ptrace(2) on unrelated PIDs, bpf(2) on unprivileged contexts).
implementation_examples: Linux auditd with syscall rules (ausearch -k privesc)
Falco runtime rules on suspicious syscall sequences
eBPF-based tools (tetragon, tracee) for kernel-level visibility
D3-SCA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Self-managed AI hosts: standard syscall monitoring applies. Serverless: equivalent is provider-side runtime telemetry (Lambda runtime API, GVisor sentry events). MCP server hosts especially: malicious MCP plugins often hit suspicious syscalls (ptrace, bpf, unshare).
D3-SCA.lag_notes: SI-4 prescribes monitoring at the system level abstractly; specific syscall analytics (which calls, which thresholds, which response) are deployment-team choices that framework audits do not sample.
D3-SCA.description_full: Analyzing system calls to determine whether a process is exhibiting unauthorized behavior.

D3-SFA.name: System File Analysis
D3-SFA.description: Monitoring critical system files (auth databases, audit configurations, init scripts, boot loaders, sudoers, SSH authorized_keys) for unauthorized modification — file integrity monitoring with cryptographic baselines and immutable-write enforcement.
implementation_examples: AIDE / Tripwire / OSSEC file-integrity baselines on /etc, /usr/bin, /sbin
Linux IMA-EVM measured boot extending into runtime FIM
AWS Config rules monitoring IAM policy file analogs (managed-policy versions)
D3-SFA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Self-managed AI hosts: standard FIM applies to MCP server configs, ~/.claude, ~/.cursor settings. Serverless: equivalent is image-immutability + read-only rootfs (modifications outside writable tmpfs are structurally impossible).
D3-SFA.lag_notes: SI-7 covers software/firmware integrity; user-space configuration FIM is implicit not explicit. Framework audits accept 'FIM is deployed' without sampling whether the rule set covers AI-assistant config paths that have become high-value targets.
D3-SFA.description_full: Monitoring system files such as authentication databases, configuration files, system logs, and system executables for modification or tampering.

D3-EFA.name: Executable File Analysis
D3-EFA.description: Analyzing the format, contents, or static characteristics of an executable file to determine whether it warrants further investigation. Covers PE/ELF/Mach-O header inspection, embedded-string + import-table review, entropy + packer detection, and YARA-rule matching against known malicious patterns. Distinct from D3-DA (Dynamic Analysis): no execution occurs.
implementation_examples: Sigstore cosign verify on container image manifests
OEM firmware-image signature verification at provisioning time
D3-EFA.ai_pipeline_applicability: Directly applicable to model-artifact ingestion paths (pickle/safetensors/ONNX): static analysis of serialized weights can surface malicious __reduce__ payloads (D3-EFA on the pickle stream) before any deserialization occurs. For MCP-server binaries shipped via npm/PyPI, D3-EFA pairs with D3-EAL — analyze first to gate the allowlist decision rather than allow-by-publisher.
D3-EFA.lag_notes: NIST SI-3 prescribes "malicious code protection" without binding the control to static-file-analysis specifically; auditors routinely accept signature-AV deployment as the entire SI-3 implementation, missing the analyze-before-load posture that catches packed / encoder-obfuscated payloads. Distinct from D3-EAL: allowlisting blocks a binary at execute-time; D3-EFA inspects the bytes at file-write / image-pull / artifact-fetch time and gates the allowlist itself.
D3-EFA.description_full: Emulating instructions in a file looking for specific patterns.

D3-FMCV.name: File Metadata Consistency Validation
D3-FMCV.description: The process of validating the consistency between a file's metadata and its actual content, ensuring that elements like declared lengths, pointers, and checksums accurately describe the file's content.
D3-FMCV.description_full: The process of validating the consistency between a file's metadata and its actual content, ensuring that elements like declared lengths, pointers, and checksums accurately describe the file's content.

D3-FMVV.name: File Metadata Value Verification
D3-FMVV.description: The process of checking specific static values within a file, such as file signatures or magic numbers, to ensure they match the expected values defined by the file format specification.
D3-FMVV.description_full: The process of checking specific static values within a file, such as file signatures or magic numbers, to ensure they match the expected values defined by the file format specification.

D3-FRDDL.name: Forward Resolution Domain Denylisting
D3-FRDDL.description: Blocking a lookup based on the query's domain name value.
D3-FRDDL.description_full: Blocking a lookup based on the query's domain name value.

D3-FRIDL.name: Forward Resolution IP Denylisting
D3-FRIDL.description: Blocking a DNS lookup's answer's IP address value.
D3-FRIDL.description_full: Blocking a DNS lookup's answer's IP address value.

D3-HBPI.name: Hardware-based Process Isolation
D3-HBPI.description: Preventing one process from writing to the memory space of another process through hardware based address manager implementations.
D3-HBPI.description_full: Preventing one process from writing to the memory space of another process through hardware based address manager implementations.

D3-HBWP.name: Hardware-based Write Protection
D3-HBWP.description: Physical methods of preventing data from being written to computer storage.
D3-HBWP.description_full: Physical methods of preventing data from being written to computer storage.

D3-HCI.name: Hardware Component Inventory
D3-HCI.description: Hardware component inventorying identifies and records the hardware items in the organization's architecture.
D3-HCI.description_full: Hardware component inventorying identifies and records the hardware items in the organization's architecture.

D3-HD.name: Homoglyph Detection
D3-HD.description: Comparing strings using a variety of techniques to determine if a deceptive or malicious string is being presented to a user.
D3-HD.description_full: Comparing strings using a variety of techniques to determine if a deceptive or malicious string is being presented to a user.

D3-HDDL.name: Hierarchical Domain Denylisting
D3-HDDL.description: Blocking the resolution of any subdomain of a specified domain name.
D3-HDDL.description_full: Blocking the resolution of any subdomain of a specified domain name.

D3-HDL.name: Homoglyph Denylisting
D3-HDL.description: Blocking DNS queries that are deceptively similar to legitimate domain names.
D3-HDL.description_full: Blocking DNS queries that are deceptively similar to legitimate domain names.

D3-HR.name: Host Reboot
D3-HR.description: Initiating a host's reboot sequence to terminate all running processes.
D3-HR.description_full: Initiating a host's reboot sequence to terminate all running processes.

D3-HS.name: Host Shutdown
D3-HS.description: Initiating a host's shutdown sequence to terminate all running processes.
D3-HS.description_full: Initiating a host's shutdown sequence to terminate all running processes.

D3-IAA.name: Identifier Activity Analysis
D3-IAA.description: Taking known malicious identifiers and determining if they are present in a system.
D3-IAA.description_full: Taking known malicious identifiers and determining if they are present in a system.

D3-IBCA.name: Indirect Branch Call Analysis
D3-IBCA.description: Analyzing vendor specific branch call recording in order to detect ROP style attacks.
D3-IBCA.description_full: Analyzing vendor specific branch call recording in order to detect ROP style attacks.

D3-ID.name: Identifier Analysis
D3-ID.description: Analyzing identifier artifacts such as IP address, domain names, or URL(I)s.
D3-ID.description_full: Analyzing identifier artifacts such as IP address, domain names, or URL(I)s.

D3-IDA.name: Input Device Analysis
D3-IDA.description: Operating system level mechanisms to prevent abusive input device exploitation.
D3-IDA.description_full: Operating system level mechanisms to prevent abusive input device exploitation.

D3-IHN.name: Integrated Honeynet
D3-IHN.description: The practice of setting decoys in a production environment to entice interaction from attackers.
D3-IHN.description_full: The practice of setting decoys in a production environment to entice interaction from attackers.

D3-IPCTA.name: IPC Traffic Analysis
D3-IPCTA.description: Analyzing standard inter process communication (IPC) protocols to detect deviations from normal protocol activity.
D3-IPCTA.description_full: Analyzing standard inter process communication (IPC) protocols to detect deviations from normal protocol activity.

D3-IPRA.name: IP Reputation Analysis
D3-IPRA.description: Analyzing the reputation of an IP address.
D3-IPRA.description_full: Analyzing the reputation of an IP address.

D3-IRA.name: Identifier Reputation Analysis
D3-IRA.description: Analyzing the reputation of an identifier.
D3-IRA.description_full: Analyzing the reputation of an identifier.

D3-IRV.name: Integer Range Validation
D3-IRV.description: Ensuring that an integer is within a valid range.
D3-IRV.description_full: Ensuring that an integer is within a valid range.

D3-ISVA.name: Inbound Session Volume Analysis
D3-ISVA.description: Analyzing inbound network session or connection attempt volume.
D3-ISVA.description_full: Analyzing inbound network session or connection attempt volume.

D3-ITF.name: Inbound Traffic Filtering
D3-ITF.description: Restricting network traffic originating from untrusted networks destined towards a private host or enclave.
D3-ITF.description_full: Restricting network traffic originating from untrusted networks destined towards a private host or enclave.

D3-JFAPA.name: Job Function Access Pattern Analysis
D3-JFAPA.description: Detecting anomalies in user access patterns by comparing user access activity to behavioral profiles that categorize users by role such as job title, function, department.
D3-JFAPA.description_full: Detecting anomalies in user access patterns by comparing user access activity to behavioral profiles that categorize users by role such as job title, function, department.

D3-LAM.name: Local Account Monitoring
D3-LAM.description: Analyzing local user accounts to detect unauthorized activity.
D3-LAM.description_full: Analyzing local user accounts to detect unauthorized activity.

D3-LAMED.name: LAN Access Mediation
D3-LAMED.description: LAN access mediation encompasses the application of strict access control policies, systematic verification of devices, and authentication mechanisms to govern connectivity to a Local Area Network.
D3-LAMED.description_full: LAN access mediation encompasses the application of strict access control policies, systematic verification of devices, and authentication mechanisms to govern connectivity to a Local Area Network.

D3-LFAM.name: Local File Access Mediation
D3-LFAM.description: Local file access mediation is the process of an operating system granting or denying a specific access request to a local file.
D3-LFAM.description_full: Local file access mediation is the process of an operating system granting or denying a specific access request to a local file.

D3-LFP.name: Local File Permissions
D3-LFP.description: Local file permissions is the systematic process of defining, implementing, and managing access control policies that dictate user permissions for accessing files on a local system through the configuration of operating system functionality.
D3-LFP.description_full: Local file permissions is the systematic process of defining, implementing, and managing access control policies that dictate user permissions for accessing files on a local system through the configuration of operating system functionality.

D3-LLM.name: Logical Link Mapping
D3-LLM.description: Logical link mapping creates a model of existing or previous node-to-node connections using network-layer data or metadata.
D3-LLM.description_full: Logical link mapping creates a model of existing or previous node-to-node connections using network-layer data or metadata.

D3-MA.name: Message Analysis
D3-MA.description: Analyzing email or instant message content to detect unauthorized activity.
D3-MA.description_full: Analyzing email or instant message content to detect unauthorized activity.

D3-MAN.name: Message Authentication
D3-MAN.description: Authenticating the sender of a message and ensuring message integrity.
D3-MAN.description_full: Authenticating the sender of a message and ensuring message integrity.

D3-MBSV.name: Memory Block Start Validation
D3-MBSV.description: Ensuring that a pointer accurately references the beginning of a designated memory block.
D3-MBSV.description_full: Ensuring that a pointer accurately references the beginning of a designated memory block.

D3-MBT.name: Memory Boundary Tracking
D3-MBT.description: Analyzing a call stack for return addresses which point to unexpected  memory locations.
D3-MBT.description_full: Analyzing a call stack for return addresses which point to unexpected  memory locations.

D3-MH.name: Message Hardening
D3-MH.description: The application of security controls to user-to-user and system-to-system communications so messages remain confidential, unaltered, and verifiable while resisting injection, replay, and tampering.
D3-MH.description_full: The application of security controls to user-to-user and system-to-system communications so messages remain confidential, unaltered, and verifiable while resisting injection, replay, and tampering.

D3-MSM.name: Motion Sensor Monitoring
D3-MSM.description: Monitoring events from motion detectors (e.g., passive IR, microwave, dual-technology) to detect presence or movement within protected areas.
D3-MSM.description_full: Monitoring events from motion detectors (e.g., passive IR, microwave, dual-technology) to detect presence or movement within protected areas.

D3-NAM.name: Network Access Mediation
D3-NAM.description: Network access mediation is the control method for authorizing access to a system by a user (or a process acting on behalf of a user) communicating through a network, including a local area network, a wide area network, and the Internet.
D3-NAM.description_full: Network access mediation is the control method for authorizing access to a system by a user (or a process acting on behalf of a user) communicating through a network, including a local area network, a wide area network, and the Internet.

D3-NM.name: Network Mapping
D3-NM.description: Network mapping encompasses the techniques to identify and model the physical layer, network layer, and data exchange layers of the organization's network and their physical location, and determine allowed pathways through that network.
D3-NM.description_full: Network mapping encompasses the techniques to identify and model the physical layer, network layer, and data exchange layers of the organization's network and their physical location, and determine allowed pathways through that network.

D3-NNI.name: Network Node Inventory
D3-NNI.description: Network node inventorying identifies and records all the network nodes (hosts, routers, switches, firewalls, etc.) in the organization's architecture.
D3-NNI.description_full: Network node inventorying identifies and records all the network nodes (hosts, routers, switches, firewalls, etc.) in the organization's architecture.

D3-NPC.name: Null Pointer Checking
D3-NPC.description: Checking if a pointer is NULL.
D3-NPC.description_full: Checking if a pointer is NULL.

D3-NRAM.name: Network Resource Access Mediation
D3-NRAM.description: Control of access to organizational systems and services by users or processes over a network.
D3-NRAM.description_full: Control of access to organizational systems and services by users or processes over a network.

D3-NTCD.name: Network Traffic Community Deviation
D3-NTCD.description: Establishing baseline communities of network hosts and identifying statistically divergent inter-community communication.
D3-NTCD.description_full: Establishing baseline communities of network hosts and identifying statistically divergent inter-community communication.

D3-NTF.name: Network Traffic Filtering
D3-NTF.description: Restricting network traffic originating from any location.
D3-NTF.description_full: Restricting network traffic originating from any location.

D3-NTSA.name: Network Traffic Signature Analysis
D3-NTSA.description: Analyzing network traffic and compares it to known signatures.
D3-NTSA.description_full: Analyzing network traffic and compares it to known signatures

D3-NVA.name: Network Vulnerability Assessment
D3-NVA.description: Network vulnerability assessment relates all the vulnerabilities of a network's components in the context of their configuration and interdependencies and can also include assessing risk emerging from the network's design as a whole, not just the sum of individual network node or network segment vulnerabilities.
D3-NVA.description_full: Network vulnerability assessment relates all the vulnerabilities of a network's components in the context of their configuration and interdependencies and can also include assessing risk emerging from the network's design as a whole, not just the sum of individual network node or network segment vulnerabilities.

D3-OAM.name: Operational Activity Mapping
D3-OAM.description: Operational activity mapping identifies activities of the organization and the organization's suborganizations, groups, roles, and individuals that carry out the activities and then establishes the dependencies of the activities on the systems and people that perform those activities.
D3-OAM.description_full: Operational activity mapping identifies activities of the organization and the organization's suborganizations, groups, roles, and individuals that carry out the activities and then establishes the dependencies of the activities on the systems and people that perform those activities.

D3-ODM.name: Operational Dependency Mapping
D3-ODM.description: Operational dependency mapping identifies and models the dependencies of the organization's activities on each other and on the organization's performers (people, systems, and services.)  This may include modeling the higher- and lower-level activities of an organization forming a hierarchy, or layering, of the dependencies in an organization's activities.
D3-ODM.description_full: Operational dependency mapping identifies and models the dependencies of the organization's activities on each other and on the organization's performers (people, systems, and services.)  This may include modeling the higher- and lower-level activities of an organization forming a hierarchy, or layering, of the dependencies in an organization's activities.

D3-OE.name: Object Eviction
D3-OE.description: Terminate or remove an object from a host machine.
D3-OE.description_full: Terminate or remove an object from a host machine. This is the broadest class for object eviction.

D3-OLV.name: Operational Logic Validation
D3-OLV.description: Validation of variable state in the context of the control logic of the operational application.
D3-OLV.description_full: Validation of variable state in the context of the control logic of the operational application.

D3-OM.name: Organization Mapping
D3-OM.description: Organization mapping identifies and models the people, roles, and groups with an organization and the relations between them.
D3-OM.description_full: Organization mapping identifies and models the people, roles, and groups with an organization and the relations between them.

D3-OMM.name: Operating Mode Monitoring
D3-OMM.description: Detects operating modes such as Program, Run, Remote, or Stop.
D3-OMM.description_full: Detects operating modes such as Program, Run, Remote, or Stop.

D3-OPM.name: Operational Process Monitoring
D3-OPM.description: Monitoring physical parameters and operator actions related to an operational environment.
D3-OPM.description_full: Monitoring physical parameters and operator actions related to an operational environment.

D3-OPR.name: Operating Mode Restriction
D3-OPR.description: Restricting unauthorized changes to the operating mode prevents devices from switching into inappropriate or vulnerable states during normal use.
D3-OPR.description_full: Restricting unauthorized changes to the operating mode prevents devices from switching into inappropriate or vulnerable states during normal use.

D3-ORA.name: Operational Risk Assessment
D3-ORA.description: Operational risk assessment identifies and models the vulnerabilities of, and risks to, an organization's activities individually and as a whole.
D3-ORA.description_full: Operational risk assessment identifies and models the vulnerabilities of, and risks to, an organization's activities individually and as a whole.

D3-OSM.name: Operating System Monitoring
D3-OSM.description: The operating system software, for D3FEND's purposes, includes the kernel and its process management functions, hardware drivers, initialization or boot logic.
D3-OSM.description_full: The operating system software, for D3FEND's purposes, includes the kernel and its process management functions, hardware drivers, initialization or boot logic. It also includes and other key system daemons and their configuration. The monitoring or analysis of these components for unauthorized activity constitute **Operating System Monitoring**.

D3-OTF.name: Outbound Traffic Filtering
D3-OTF.description: Restricting network traffic originating from a private host or enclave destined towards untrusted networks.
D3-OTF.description_full: Restricting network traffic originating from a private host or enclave destined towards untrusted networks.

D3-OTP.name: One-time Password
D3-OTP.description: A one-time password is valid for only one user authentication.
D3-OTP.description_full: A one-time password is valid for only one user authentication.

D3-OVAR.name: OT Variable Access Restriction
D3-OVAR.description: Assign read/write access controls on designated registers or data tags to prevent unauthorized writes.
D3-OVAR.description_full: Assign read/write access controls on designated registers or data tags to prevent unauthorized writes.

D3-PAM.name: Physical Access Mediation
D3-PAM.description: Physical access mediation is the process of granting or denying specific requests to enter specific physical facilities (e.g., Federal buildings, military establishments, border crossing entrances.).
D3-PAM.description_full: Physical access mediation is the process of granting or denying specific requests to enter specific physical facilities (e.g., Federal buildings, military establishments, border crossing entrances.)

D3-PAN.name: Pointer Authentication
D3-PAN.description: Comparing the cryptographic hash or derivative of a pointer's value to an expected value.
D3-PAN.description_full: Comparing the cryptographic hash or derivative of a pointer's value to an expected value.

D3-PBWSAM.name: Proxy-based Web Server Access Mediation
D3-PBWSAM.description: Proxy-based web server access mediation focuses on the regulation of web server access through intermediary proxy servers.
D3-PBWSAM.description_full: Proxy-based web server access mediation focuses on the regulation of web server access through intermediary proxy servers.

D3-PCA.name: Passive Certificate Analysis
D3-PCA.description: Collecting host certificates from network traffic or other passive sources like a certificate transparency log and analyzing them for unauthorized activity.
D3-PCA.description_full: Collecting host certificates from network traffic or other passive sources like a certificate transparency log and analyzing them for unauthorized activity.

D3-PCSV.name: Process Code Segment Verification
D3-PCSV.description: Comparing the "text" or "code" memory segments to a source of truth.
D3-PCSV.description_full: Comparing the "text" or "code" memory segments to a source of truth.

D3-PE.name: Process Eviction
D3-PE.description: Process eviction techniques terminate or remove running process.
D3-PE.description_full: Process eviction techniques terminate or remove running process.

D3-PEH.name: Physical Enclosure Hardening
D3-PEH.description: Physical changes to a computer enclosure which reduce the ability for agents or the environment to affect the contained computer system.
D3-PEH.description_full: Physical changes to a computer enclosure which reduce the ability for agents or the environment to affect the contained computer system.

D3-PH.name: Platform Hardening
D3-PH.description: Hardening components of a Platform with the intention of making them more difficult to exploit.
D3-PH.description_full:
Hardening components of a Platform with the intention of making them more difficult to exploit.

Platforms includes components such as:
* BIOS UEFI Subsystems
* Hardware security devices such as Trusted Platform Modules
* Boot process logic or code
* Kernel software components

D3-PHAM.name: Physical Access Monitoring
D3-PHAM.description: Monitoring the physical access of a specified environment through detection, recording, reviewing, and logging of who/what enters and exists areas.
D3-PHAM.description_full: Monitoring the physical access of a specified environment through detection, recording, reviewing, and logging of who/what enters and exists areas.

D3-PHDURA.name: Per Host Download-Upload Ratio Analysis
D3-PHDURA.description: Detecting anomalies that indicate malicious activity by comparing the amount of data downloaded versus data uploaded by a host.
D3-PHDURA.description_full: Detecting anomalies that indicate malicious activity by comparing the amount of data downloaded versus data uploaded by a host.

D3-PLA.name: Process Lineage Analysis
D3-PLA.description: Identification of suspicious processes executing on an end-point device by examining the ancestry and siblings of a process, and the associated metadata of each node on the tree, such as process execution, duration, and order relative to siblings and ancestors.
D3-PLA.description_full: Identification of suspicious processes executing on an end-point device by examining the ancestry and siblings of a process, and the associated metadata of each node on the tree, such as process execution, duration, and order relative to siblings and ancestors.

D3-PLLM.name: Passive Logical Link Mapping
D3-PLLM.description: Passive logical link mapping only listens to network traffic as a means to map the the whole data link layer, where the links represent logical data flows rather than physical connections.
D3-PLLM.description_full: Passive logical link mapping only listens to network traffic as a means to map the the whole data link layer, where the links represent logical data flows rather than physical connections.

D3-PLM.name: Physical Link Mapping
D3-PLM.description: Physical link mapping identifies and models the link connectivity of the network devices within a physical network.
D3-PLM.description_full: Physical link mapping identifies and models the link connectivity of the network devices within a physical network.

D3-PM.name: Platform Monitoring
D3-PM.description: Monitoring platform components such as operating systems software, hardware devices, or firmware.
D3-PM.description_full: Monitoring platform components such as operating systems software, hardware devices, or firmware.

D3-PMAD.name: Protocol Metadata Anomaly Detection
D3-PMAD.description: Collecting network communication protocol metadata and identifying statistical outliers.
D3-PMAD.description_full: Collecting network communication protocol metadata and identifying statistical outliers.

D3-PR.name: Password Rotation
D3-PR.description: Password rotation is a security policy that mandates the periodic change of user account passwords to mitigate the risk of unauthorized access due to compromised credentials.
D3-PR.description_full: Password rotation is a security policy that mandates the periodic change of user account passwords to mitigate the risk of unauthorized access due to compromised credentials.

D3-PS.name: Process Suspension
D3-PS.description: Suspending a running process on a computer system.
D3-PS.description_full: Suspending a running process on a computer system.

D3-PSA.name: Process Spawn Analysis
D3-PSA.description: Analyzing spawn arguments or attributes of a process to detect processes that are unauthorized.
D3-PSA.description_full: Analyzing spawn arguments or attributes of a process to detect processes that are unauthorized.

D3-PSM.name: Proximity Sensor Monitoring
D3-PSM.description: Monitoring events from proximity sensors that indicate a credential or tagged asset is within the sensor’s read range or a defined zone.
D3-PSM.description_full: Monitoring events from proximity sensors that indicate a credential or tagged asset is within the sensor’s read range or a defined zone. Common enabling technologies include RFID, Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), and Ultra-Wideband (UWB).

D3-PSMD.name: Process Self-Modification Detection
D3-PSMD.description: Detects processes that modify, change, or replace their own code at runtime.
D3-PSMD.description_full: Detects processes that modify, change, or replace their own code at runtime.

D3-PT.name: Process Termination
D3-PT.description: Terminating a running application process on a computer system.
D3-PT.description_full: Terminating a running application process on a computer system.

D3-PUM.name: Platform Uptime Monitoring
D3-PUM.description: Monitor the amount of time since the last power cycle or restart.
D3-PUM.description_full: Monitor the amount of time since the last power cycle or restart.

D3-PV.name: Pointer Validation
D3-PV.description: Ensuring that a pointer variable has the required properties for use.
D3-PV.description_full: Ensuring that a pointer variable has the required properties for use.

D3-PWA.name: Password Authentication
D3-PWA.description: Password authentication is a security mechanism used to verify the identity of a user or entity attempting to access a system or resource by requiring the input of a secret string of characters, known as a password, that is associated with the user or entity.
D3-PWA.description_full: Password authentication is a security mechanism used to verify the identity of a user or entity attempting to access a system or resource by requiring the input of a secret string of characters, known as a password, that is associated with the user or entity.

D3-RA.name: Restore Access
D3-RA.description: Restoring an entity's access to resources.
D3-RA.description_full: Restoring an entity's access to resources.

D3-RAM.name: Routing Access Mediation
D3-RAM.description: Routing access mediation is a network security approach that manages and controls access at the network layer using VPNs, tunneling protocols, firewall rules, and traffic inspection to ensure secure and efficient data routing.
D3-RAM.description_full: Routing access mediation is a network security approach that manages and controls access at the network layer using VPNs, tunneling protocols, firewall rules, and traffic inspection to ensure secure and efficient data routing.

D3-RAPA.name: Resource Access Pattern Analysis
D3-RAPA.description: Analyzing the resources accessed by a user to identify unauthorized activity.
D3-RAPA.description_full: Analyzing the resources accessed by a user to identify unauthorized activity.

D3-RD.name: Restore Database
D3-RD.description: Restoring the data in a database.
D3-RD.description_full: Restoring the data in a database.

D3-RDI.name: Restore Disk Image
D3-RDI.description: Restoring a previously captured disk image a hard drive.
D3-RDI.description_full: Restoring a previously captured disk image a hard drive.

D3-RE.name: Restore Email
D3-RE.description: Restoring an email for an entity to access.
D3-RE.description_full: Restoring an email for an entity to access.

D3-RF.name: Restore File
D3-RF.description: Restoring a file for an entity to access.
D3-RF.description_full: Restoring a file for an entity to access.

D3-RFAM.name: Remote File Access Mediation
D3-RFAM.description: Remote file access mediation is the process of managing and securing access to file systems over a network to ensure that only authorized users or processes can interact with remote files.
D3-RFAM.description_full: Remote file access mediation is the process of managing and securing access to file systems over a network to ensure that only authorized users or processes can interact with remote files.

D3-RFS.name: RF Shielding
D3-RFS.description: Adding physical barriers to a platform to prevent undesired radio interference.
D3-RFS.description_full: Adding physical barriers to a platform to prevent undesired radio interference.

D3-RFUM.name: Remote Firmware Update Monitoring
D3-RFUM.description: Monitoring of remote firmware update commands to identify unauthorized software installations.
D3-RFUM.description_full: Monitoring of remote firmware update commands to identify unauthorized software installations.

D3-RH.name: Radiation Hardening
D3-RH.description: Radiation hardening is the process of making electronic components and circuits resistant to damage or malfunction caused by high levels of ionizing radiation.
D3-RH.description_full: Radiation hardening is the process of making electronic components and circuits resistant to damage or malfunction caused by high levels of ionizing radiation.

D3-RIC.name: Reissue Credential
D3-RIC.description: Issue a new credential to a user which supercedes their old credential.
D3-RIC.description_full: Issue a new credential to a user which supercedes their old credential.

D3-RN.name: Reference Nullification
D3-RN.description: Invalidating all pointers that reference a specific memory block, ensuring that the block cannot be accessed or modified after deallocation.
D3-RN.description_full: Invalidating all pointers that reference a specific memory block, ensuring that the block cannot be accessed or modified after deallocation.

D3-RNA.name: Restore Network Access
D3-RNA.description: Restoring a entity's access to a computer network.
D3-RNA.description_full: Restoring a entity's access to a computer network.

D3-RO.name: Restore Object
D3-RO.description: Restoring an object for an entity to access.
D3-RO.description_full: Restoring an object for an entity to access. This is the broadest class for object restoral.

D3-RRID.name: Reverse Resolution IP Denylisting
D3-RRID.description: Blocking a reverse lookup based on the query's IP address value.
D3-RRID.description_full: Blocking a reverse lookup based on the query's IP address value.

D3-RTA.name: RPC Traffic Analysis
D3-RTA.description: Monitoring the activity of remote procedure calls in communication traffic to establish standard protocol operations and potential attacker activities.
D3-RTA.description_full: Monitoring the activity of remote procedure calls in communication traffic to establish standard protocol operations and potential attacker activities.

D3-RTSD.name: Remote Terminal Session Detection
D3-RTSD.description: Detection of an unauthorized remote live terminal console session by examining network traffic to a network host.
D3-RTSD.description_full: Detection of an unauthorized remote live terminal console session by examining network traffic to a network host.

D3-RUAA.name: Restore User Account Access
D3-RUAA.description: Restoring a user account's access to resources.
D3-RUAA.description_full: Restoring a user account's access to resources.

D3-SAOR.name: Segment Address Offset Randomization
D3-SAOR.description: Randomizing the base (start) address of one or more segments of memory during the initialization of a process.
D3-SAOR.description_full: Randomizing the base (start) address of one or more segments of memory during the initialization of a process.

D3-SBV.name: Service Binary Verification
D3-SBV.description: Analyzing changes in service binary files by comparing to a source of truth.
D3-SBV.description_full: Analyzing changes in service binary files by comparing to a source of truth.

D3-SCF.name: System Call Filtering
D3-SCF.description: Controlling access to local computer system resources with kernel-level capabilities.
D3-SCF.description_full: Controlling access to local computer system resources with kernel-level capabilities.

D3-SCH.name: Source Code Hardening
D3-SCH.description: Hardening source code with the intention of making it more difficult to exploit and less error prone.
D3-SCH.description_full: Hardening source code with the intention of making it more difficult to exploit and less error prone.

D3-SDA.name: Session Duration Analysis
D3-SDA.description: Analyzing the duration of user sessions in order to detect unauthorized  activity.
D3-SDA.description_full: Analyzing the duration of user sessions in order to detect unauthorized  activity.

D3-SDM.name: System Daemon Monitoring
D3-SDM.description: Tracking changes to the state or configuration of critical system level processes.
D3-SDM.description_full: Tracking changes to the state or configuration of critical system level processes.

D3-SEA.name: Script Execution Analysis
D3-SEA.description: Analyzing the execution of a script to detect unauthorized user activity.
D3-SEA.description_full: Analyzing the execution of a script to detect unauthorized user activity.

D3-SFCV.name: Stack Frame Canary Validation
D3-SFCV.description: Comparing a value stored in a stack frame with a known good value in order to prevent or detect a memory segment overwrite.
D3-SFCV.description_full: Comparing a value stored in a stack frame with a known good value in order to prevent or detect a memory segment overwrite.

D3-SFV.name: System Firmware Verification
D3-SFV.description: Cryptographically verifying installed system firmware integrity.
D3-SFV.description_full: Cryptographically verifying installed system firmware integrity.

D3-SHN.name: Standalone Honeynet
D3-SHN.description: An environment created for the purpose of attracting attackers and eliciting their behaviors that is not connected to any production enterprise systems.
D3-SHN.description_full: An environment created for the purpose of attracting attackers and eliciting their behaviors that is not connected to any production enterprise systems.

D3-SICA.name: System Init Config Analysis
D3-SICA.description: Analysis of any system process startup configuration.
D3-SICA.description_full: Analysis of any system process startup configuration.

D3-SJA.name: Scheduled Job Analysis
D3-SJA.description: Analysis of source files, processes, destination files, or destination servers associated with a scheduled job to detect unauthorized use of job scheduling.
D3-SJA.description_full: Analysis of source files, processes, destination files, or destination servers associated with a scheduled job to detect unauthorized use of job scheduling.

D3-SMRA.name: Sender MTA Reputation Analysis
D3-SMRA.description: Characterizing the reputation of mail transfer agents (MTA) to determine the security risk in emails.
D3-SMRA.description_full: Characterizing the reputation of mail transfer agents (MTA) to determine the security risk in emails.

D3-SPP.name: Strong Password Policy
D3-SPP.description: Modifying system configuration to increase password strength.
D3-SPP.description_full: Modifying system configuration to increase password strength.

D3-SRA.name: Sender Reputation Analysis
D3-SRA.description: Ascertaining sender reputation based on information associated with a message (e.g.
D3-SRA.description_full: Ascertaining sender reputation based on information associated with a message (e.g. email/instant messaging).

D3-SSC.name: Shadow Stack Comparisons
D3-SSC.description: Comparing a call stack in system memory with a shadow call stack maintained by the processor to determine unauthorized shellcode activity.
D3-SSC.description_full: Comparing a call stack in system memory with a shadow call stack maintained by the processor to determine unauthorized shellcode activity.

D3-ST.name: Session Termination
D3-ST.description: Forcefully end all active sessions associated with compromised accounts or devices.
D3-ST.description_full: Forcefully end all active sessions associated with compromised accounts or devices.

D3-SU.name: Software Update
D3-SU.description: Replacing old software on a computer system component.
D3-SU.description_full: Replacing old software on a computer system component.

D3-SVCDM.name: Service Dependency Mapping
D3-SVCDM.description: Service dependency mapping determines the services on which each given service relies.
D3-SVCDM.description_full: Service dependency mapping determines the services on which each given service relies.

D3-SWI.name: Software Inventory
D3-SWI.description: Software inventorying identifies and records the software items in the organization's architecture.
D3-SWI.description_full: Software inventorying identifies and records the software items in the organization's architecture.

D3-SYSDM.name: System Dependency Mapping
D3-SYSDM.description: System dependency mapping identifies and models the dependencies of system components on each other to carry out their function.
D3-SYSDM.description_full: System dependency mapping identifies and models the dependencies of system components on each other to carry out their function.

D3-SYSM.name: System Mapping
D3-SYSM.description: System mapping encompasses the techniques to identify the organization's systems, how they are configured and decomposed into subsystems and components, how they are dependent on one another, and where they are physically located.
D3-SYSM.description_full: System mapping encompasses the techniques to identify the organization's systems, how they are configured and decomposed into subsystems and components, how they are dependent on one another, and where they are physically located.

D3-SYSVA.name: System Vulnerability Assessment
D3-SYSVA.description: System vulnerability assessment relates all the vulnerabilities of a system's components in the context of their configuration and internal dependencies and can also include assessing risk emerging from the system's design as a whole, not just the sum of individual component vulnerabilities.
D3-SYSVA.description_full: System vulnerability assessment relates all the vulnerabilities of a system's components in the context of their configuration and internal dependencies and can also include assessing risk emerging from the system's design as a whole, not just the sum of individual component vulnerabilities.

D3-TAAN.name: Transfer Agent Authentication
D3-TAAN.description: Validating that server components of a messaging infrastructure are authorized to send a particular message.
D3-TAAN.description_full: Validating that server components of a messaging infrastructure are authorized to send a particular message.

D3-TB.name: Token Binding
D3-TB.description: Token binding is a security mechanism used to enhance the protection of tokens, such as cookies or OAuth tokens, by binding them to a specific connection.
D3-TB.description_full: Token binding is a security mechanism used to enhance the protection of tokens, such as cookies or OAuth tokens, by binding them to a specific connection.

D3-TBA.name: Token-based Authentication
D3-TBA.description: Token-based authentication is an authentication protocol where users verify their identity in exchange for a unique access token.
D3-TBA.description_full: Token-based authentication is an authentication protocol where users verify their identity in exchange for a unique access token. Users can then access the website, application, or resource for the life of the token without having to re-enter their credentials.

D3-TBI.name: TPM Boot Integrity
D3-TBI.description: Assuring the integrity of a platform by demonstrating that the boot process starts from a trusted combination of hardware and software and continues until the operating system has fully booted and applications are running.
D3-TBI.description_full: Assuring the integrity of a platform by demonstrating that the boot process starts from a trusted combination of hardware and software and continues until the operating system has fully booted and applications are running.  Sometimes called Static Root of Trust Measurement (STRM).

D3-TL.name: Trusted Library
D3-TL.description: A trusted library is a collection of pre-verified and secure code modules or components that are used within software applications to perform specific functions.
D3-TL.description_full: A trusted library is a collection of pre-verified and secure code modules or components that are used within software applications to perform specific functions. These libraries are considered reliable and have been vetted for security vulnerabilities, ensuring they do not introduce risks into the application.

D3-UA.name: URL Analysis
D3-UA.description: Determining if a URL is benign or malicious by analyzing the URL or its components.
D3-UA.description_full: Determining if a URL is benign or malicious by analyzing the URL or its components.

D3-UAP.name: User Account Permissions
D3-UAP.description: Restricting a user account's access to resources.
D3-UAP.description_full: Restricting a user account's access to resources.

D3-UBA.name: User Behavior Analysis
D3-UBA.description: User behavior analytics ("UBA") as defined by Gartner, is a cybersecurity process about detection of insider threats, targeted attacks, and financial fraud.
D3-UBA.description_full: User behavior analytics ("UBA") as defined by Gartner, is a cybersecurity process about detection of insider threats, targeted attacks, and financial fraud. UBA solutions look at patterns of human behavior, and then apply algorithms and statistical analysis to detect meaningful anomalies from those patterns-anomalies that indicate potential threats.' Instead of tracking devices or security events, UBA tracks a system's users. Big data platforms are increasing UBA functionality by allowing them to analyze petabytes worth of data to detect insider threats and advanced persistent threats.

D3-UDTA.name: User Data Transfer Analysis
D3-UDTA.description: Analyzing the amount of data transferred by a user.
D3-UDTA.description_full: Analyzing the amount of data transferred by a user.

D3-UGLPA.name: User Geolocation Logon Pattern Analysis
D3-UGLPA.description: Monitoring geolocation data of user logon attempts and comparing it to a baseline user behavior profile to identify anomalies in logon location.
D3-UGLPA.description_full: Monitoring geolocation data of user logon attempts and comparing it to a baseline user behavior profile to identify anomalies in logon location.

D3-ULA.name: Unlock Account
D3-ULA.description: Restoring a user account's access to resources by unlocking a locked User Account.
D3-ULA.description_full: Restoring a user account's access to resources by unlocking a locked User Account.

D3-URA.name: URL Reputation Analysis
D3-URA.description: Analyzing the reputation of a URL.
D3-URA.description_full: Analyzing the reputation of a URL.

D3-USICA.name: User Session Init Config Analysis
D3-USICA.description: Analyzing modifications to user session config files such as .bashrc or .bash_profile.
D3-USICA.description_full: Analyzing modifications to user session config files such as .bashrc or .bash_profile.

D3-VI.name: Variable Initialization
D3-VI.description: Setting variables to a known value before use.
D3-VI.description_full: Setting variables to a known value before use.
