D3-VS.name: Video Surveillance
D3-VS.description: Monitoring of physical areas via camera video feeds to deter, detect, and investigate unauthorized access and related security events.
D3-VS.description_full: Monitoring of physical areas via camera video feeds to deter, detect, and investigate unauthorized access and related security events.

D3-VTV.name: Variable Type Validation
D3-VTV.description: Ensuring that a variable has the correct type.
D3-VTV.description_full: Ensuring that a variable has the correct type.

D3-WSAA.name: Web Session Activity Analysis
D3-WSAA.description: Monitoring changes in user web session behavior by comparing current web session activity to a baseline behavior profile or a catalog of predetermined malicious behavior.
D3-WSAA.description_full: Monitoring changes in user web session behavior by comparing current web session activity to a baseline behavior profile or a catalog of predetermined malicious behavior.

D3-WSAM.name: Web Session Access Mediation
D3-WSAM.description: Web session access mediation secures user sessions in web applications by employing robust authentication and integrity validation, along with adaptive threat mitigation techniques, to ensure that access to web resources is authorized and protected from session-related attacks.
D3-WSAM.description_full: Web session access mediation secures user sessions in web applications by employing robust authentication and integrity validation, along with adaptive threat mitigation techniques, to ensure that access to web resources is authorized and protected from session-related attacks.

D3A-AAD.name: Average Absolute Deviation
D3A-AAD.description: The average absolute deviation (AAD) of a data set is the average of the absolute deviations from a central point.
D3A-AAD.description_full: The average absolute deviation (AAD) of a data set is the average of the absolute deviations from a central point.

D3A-ABC.name: ANN-based Clustering
D3A-ABC.description: Combines the principles of Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and clustering methods.
D3A-ABC.description_full: Combines the principles of Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and clustering methods.

D3A-AC.name: Agglomerative Clustering
D3A-AC.description: Agglomerative Clustering is a type of hierarchical clustering method where data points are grouped together based on similarity.
D3A-AC.description_full: Agglomerative Clustering is a type of hierarchical clustering method where data points are grouped together based on similarity. Initially, each data point is treated as an individual cluster, and then in successive iterations, the closest clusters are merged until only one large cluster remains or until a specified stopping criterion is met.

D3A-AFTL.name: Asymmetric Feature-based Transfer Learning
D3A-AFTL.description: Homogeneous (where the metrics are the same for both source and target) asymmetric transformation mapping transforms the source feature space to align with that of the target or the target to that of the source.
D3A-AFTL.description_full: Homogeneous (where the metrics are the same for both source and target) asymmetric transformation mapping transforms the source feature space to align with that of the target or the target to that of the source. This, in effect, bridges the feature space gap and reduces the problem into a homogeneous transfer problem when further distribution differences need to be corrected.

D3A-AL.name: Alethic Logic
D3A-AL.description: Alethic logic is a modal logic that addresses the modalities of necessity and possibility.
D3A-AL.description_full: Alethic logic is a modal logic that addresses the modalities of necessity and possibility.

D3A-AM.name: ARIMA Model
D3A-AM.description: An autoregressive integrated moving average (ARIMA) model is a generalization of an autoregressive moving average (ARMA) model.
D3A-AM.description_full: An autoregressive integrated moving average (ARIMA) model is a generalization of an autoregressive moving average (ARMA) model.

D3A-ARL.name: Association Rule Learning
D3A-ARL.description: Association rule learning is a rule-based machine learning method for discovering interesting relations between variables in large databases.
D3A-ARL.description_full: Association rule learning is a rule-based machine learning method for discovering interesting relations between variables in large databases.

D3A-ARTC.name: Adaptive Resonance Theory Clustering
D3A-ARTC.description: Adaptive Resonance Theory (ART) Clustering is a  neural network algorithm used for clustering data and is open to new learning(i.e.
D3A-ARTC.description_full: Adaptive Resonance Theory (ART) Clustering is a  neural network algorithm used for clustering data and is open to new learning(i.e. adaptive) without discarding the previous or the old information(i.e. resonance).

D3A-ASM.name: Approximate String Matching
D3A-ASM.description: Approximate string matching is a form of string matching that allows errrors.
D3A-ASM.description_full: Approximate string matching is a form of string matching that allows errrors.

D3A-ASP.name: Answer Set Programming
D3A-ASP.description: Answer set programming is a form of declarative programming based on the stable model (answer set) semantics of logic programming.
D3A-ASP.description_full: Answer set programming is a form of declarative programming based on the stable model (answer set) semantics of logic programming.

D3A-AUT.name: Autoencoding
D3A-AUT.description: Autoencoders are specific type of deep learning architecture used for learning representation of data, typically for the purpose of dimensionality reduction.
D3A-AUT.description_full: Autoencoders are specific type of deep learning architecture used for learning representation of data, typically for the purpose of dimensionality reduction. This is achieved by designing deep learning architecture that aims that copying input layer at its output layer.

D3A-BA.name: Bootstrap Aggregating
D3A-BA.description: Bootstrap aggregating, also called bagging (from bootstrap aggregating), is a machine learning ensemble meta-algorithm designed to improve the stability and accuracy of machine learning algorithms used in statistical classification and regression.
D3A-BA.description_full: Bootstrap aggregating, also called bagging (from bootstrap aggregating), is a machine learning ensemble meta-algorithm designed to improve the stability and accuracy of machine learning algorithms used in statistical classification and regression. It also reduces variance and helps to avoid overfitting. Although it is usually applied to decision tree methods, it can be used with any type of method. Bagging is a special case of the model averaging approach.

D3A-BE.name: Bayesian Estimation
D3A-BE.description: A Bayes estimator or a Bayes action is an estimator or decision rule that minimizes the posterior expected value of a loss function (i.e., the posterior expected loss).
D3A-BE.description_full: A Bayes estimator or a Bayes action is an estimator or decision rule that minimizes the posterior expected value of a loss function (i.e., the posterior expected loss).

D3A-BEM.name: Boolean Expression Matching
D3A-BEM.description: Boolean expression matching produces a Boolean truth value for a given boolean expression and assignment of values to variables in the expression.
D3A-BEM.description_full: Boolean expression matching produces a Boolean truth value for a given boolean expression and assignment of values to variables in the expression.

D3A-BER.name: BERT
D3A-BER.description: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) is based on a deep learning model in which every output element is connected to every input element, and the weightings between them are dynamically calculated based upon their connection.
D3A-BER.description_full: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) is based on a deep learning model in which every output element is connected to every input element, and the weightings between them are dynamically calculated based upon their connection.

D3A-BHT.name: Bayesian Hypothesis Testing
D3A-BHT.description: Bayesian hypothesis testing can be framed as a special case of model comparison where a model refers to a likelihood function and a prior distribution.
D3A-BHT.description_full: Bayesian hypothesis testing can be framed as a special case of model comparison where a model refers to a likelihood function and a prior distribution.

D3A-BLR.name: Bayesian Linear Regression
D3A-BLR.description: Bayesian linear regression is a type of conditional modeling in which the mean of one variable is described by a linear combination of other variables, with the goal of obtaining the posterior probability of the regression coefficients.
D3A-BLR.description_full: Bayesian linear regression is a type of conditional modeling in which the mean of one variable is described by a linear combination of other variables, with the goal of obtaining the posterior probability of the regression coefficients.

D3A-BLRL.name: Bayesian Linear Regression Learning
D3A-BLRL.description: A supervised learning method that builds a Bayesian linear regression model using training data.
D3A-BLRL.description_full: A supervised learning method that builds a Bayesian linear regression model using training data.

D3A-BM.name: Bayesian Method
D3A-BM.description: Bayesian analysis is a statistical procedure which endeavors to estimate parameters of an underlying distribution based on the observed distribution.
D3A-BM.description_full: Bayesian analysis is a statistical procedure which endeavors to estimate parameters of an underlying distribution based on the observed distribution.

D3A-BMA.name: Bayesian Model Averaging
D3A-BMA.description: A parameter estimate (or a prediction of new observations) obtained by averaging the estimates (or predictions) of the different models under consideration, each weighted by its model probability.
D3A-BMA.description_full: A parameter estimate (or a prediction of new observations) obtained by averaging the estimates (or predictions) of the different models under consideration, each weighted by its model probability.

D3A-BMC.name: Bayesian Model Combination
D3A-BMC.description: Bayesian model combination (BMC) is an algorithmic correction to Bayesian model averaging (BMA).
D3A-BMC.description_full: Bayesian model combination (BMC) is an algorithmic correction to Bayesian model averaging (BMA). Instead of sampling each model in the ensemble individually, it samples from the space of possible ensembles (with model weights drawn randomly from a Dirichlet distribution having uniform parameters)

D3A-BOC.name: Bayes Optimal Classifier
D3A-BOC.description: A probabilistic model that makes the most probable prediction for a new example.
D3A-BOC.description_full: A probabilistic model that makes the most probable prediction for a new example.

D3A-BOM.name: Bucket of Models
D3A-BOM.description: A "bucket of models" is an ensemble technique in which a model selection algorithm is used to choose the best model for each problem.
D3A-BOM.description_full: A "bucket of models" is an ensemble technique in which a model selection algorithm is used to choose the best model for each problem. When tested with only one problem, a bucket of models can produce no better results than the best model in the set, but when evaluated across many problems, it will typically produce much better results, on average, than any model in the set.

D3A-BOO.name: Boosting
D3A-BOO.description: Boosting is a sequential process where each subsequent model attempts to correct the errors of the previous model.
D3A-BOO.description_full: Boosting is a sequential process where each subsequent model attempts to correct the errors of the previous model

D3A-C4..name: C4.5
D3A-C4..description: C4.5 is an algorithm that is strongly based off ID3.
D3A-C4..description_full: C4.5 is an algorithm that is strongly based off ID3. It creates decision trees the same way as ID3. C4.5 improves on several aspects of ID3, including handling discreet variables, handling training data with missing values, and has the ability to automatically prune the decision trees it creates.

D3A-C5..name: C5.0
D3A-C5..description: C5.0 is the next version of C4.5, which in turn is the upgrade from ID3.
D3A-C5..description_full: C5.0 is the next version of C4.5, which in turn is the upgrade from ID3. The only difference between C5.0 and C4.5 is some improvements made to C5.0.

D3A-CA.name: Cluster Analysis
D3A-CA.description: Cluster analysis or clustering is the task of grouping a set of objects in such a way that objects in the same group (called a cluster) are more similar (in some sense) to each other than to those in other groups (clusters).
D3A-CA.description_full: Cluster analysis or clustering is the task of grouping a set of objects in such a way that objects in the same group (called a cluster) are more similar (in some sense) to each other than to those in other groups (clusters).

D3A-CAR.name: CART
D3A-CAR.description: The CART algorithm is a type of classification algorithm that is required to build a decision tree on the basis of Gini’s impurity index.
D3A-CAR.description_full: The CART algorithm is a type of classification algorithm that is required to build a decision tree on the basis of Gini’s impurity index.

D3A-CBC.name: Centroid-based Clustering
D3A-CBC.description: Centroid-based clustering organizes the data into non-hierarchical clusters, in contrast to hierarchical clustering defined below.
D3A-CBC.description_full: Centroid-based clustering organizes the data into non-hierarchical clusters, in contrast to hierarchical clustering defined below. K-means is the most widely-used centroid-based clustering algorithm. Centroid-based algorithms are efficient but sensitive to initial conditions and outliers.

D3A-CC.name: Canopy Clustering
D3A-CC.description: The canopy clustering algorithm is an unsupervised pre-clustering algorithm  often used as preprocessing step for the K-means algorithm or the Hierarchical clustering algorithm.
D3A-CC.description_full: The canopy clustering algorithm is an unsupervised pre-clustering algorithm  often used as preprocessing step for the K-means algorithm or the Hierarchical clustering algorithm. It is intended to speed up clustering operations on large data sets.

D3A-CLA.name: Classification
D3A-CLA.description: Classification uses an algorithm to accurately assign test data into specific categories.
D3A-CLA.description_full: Classification uses an algorithm to accurately assign test data into specific categories.

D3A-CNN.name: Convolutional Neural Network
D3A-CNN.description: A class of artificial neural network most commonly applied to analyze visual imagery.CNNs use a mathematical operation called convolution in place of general matrix multiplication in at least one of their layers.
D3A-CNN.description_full: A class of artificial neural network most commonly applied to analyze visual imagery.CNNs use a mathematical operation called convolution in place of general matrix multiplication in at least one of their layers.

D3A-COR.name: Correlation
D3A-COR.description: Correlation is the degree to which two or more quantities are linearly associated.
D3A-COR.description_full: Correlation is the degree to which two or more quantities are linearly associated.

D3A-COV.name: Coefficient of Variation
D3A-COV.description: The coefficient of variation (CV), also known as relative standard deviation (RSD), is a standardized measure of dispersion of a probability distribution or frequency distribution.
D3A-COV.description_full:
The coefficient of variation (CV), also known as relative standard deviation (RSD), is a standardized measure of dispersion of a probability distribution or frequency distribution.

The coefficient of variation (CV) is defined as the ratio of the standard deviation to the mean .

D3A-CT.name: Central Tendency
D3A-CT.description: A measure of central tendency ) is a summary measure that attempts to describe a whole set of data with a single value that represents the middle or centre of its distribution.
D3A-CT.description_full: A measure of central tendency ) is a summary measure that attempts to describe a whole set of data with a single value that represents the middle or centre of its distribution.

D3A-CV.name: Cramer's V
D3A-CV.description: Cramér's V (sometimes referred to as Cramér's phi and denoted as φc) is a measure of association between two nominal variables, giving a value between 0 and +1 (inclusive) and is based on Pearson's chi-squared statistic.
D3A-CV.description_full: Cramér's V (sometimes referred to as Cramér's phi and denoted as φc) is a measure of association between two nominal variables, giving a value between 0 and +1 (inclusive) and is based on Pearson's chi-squared statistic.

D3A-CYC.name: CycleGAN
D3A-CYC.description: The Cycle Generative Adversarial Network (CycleGAN) is an approach to training a deep convolutional neural network for image-to-image translation tasks by mapping between input and output images using unpaired dataset.
D3A-CYC.description_full: The Cycle Generative Adversarial Network (CycleGAN) is an approach to training a deep convolutional neural network for image-to-image translation tasks by mapping between input and output images using unpaired dataset.

D3A-DA.name: Discriminant Analysis
D3A-DA.description: Discriminant analysis attempts to establish whether a set of variables can be used to distinguish between two or more groups of cases.
D3A-DA.description_full: Discriminant analysis attempts to establish whether a set of variables can be used to distinguish between two or more groups of cases.

D3A-DAT.name: Datalog
D3A-DAT.description: Datalog is a declarative logic programming language that is a syntactically a subset of Prolog.
D3A-DAT.description_full: Datalog is a declarative logic programming language that is a syntactically a subset of Prolog.

D3A-DBC.name: DNN-based Clustering
D3A-DBC.description: DNNs serve for clustering as mappings to better representations.
D3A-DBC.description_full: DNNs serve for clustering as mappings to better representations. The features of these representations can be drawn from different layers of the network or even from several layers.

D3A-DBS.name: DBSCAN
D3A-DBS.description: A density-based clustering algorithm that works on the assumption that clusters are dense regions in space separated by regions of lower density.
D3A-DBS.description_full: A density-based clustering algorithm that works on the assumption that clusters are dense regions in space separated by regions of lower density.

D3A-DC.name: Divisive Clustering
D3A-DC.description: A divisive clustering approach is a hierarchical, top-down approach to clustering a dataset.
D3A-DC.description_full: A divisive clustering approach is a hierarchical, top-down approach to clustering a dataset.

D3A-DCG.name: Deep Convolutional GAN
D3A-DCG.description: Deep Convolutional GAN (DCGAN) uses convolutional and convolutional-transpose layers in the generator and discriminator, respectively.
D3A-DCG.description_full: Deep Convolutional GAN (DCGAN) uses convolutional and convolutional-transpose layers in the generator and discriminator, respectively.

D3A-DL.name: Deontic Logic
D3A-DL.description: Deontic logic addresses the modality of obligations and norms; i.e., the modality of morality.
D3A-DL.description_full: Deontic logic addresses the modality of obligations and norms; i.e., the modality of morality.

D3A-DNNC.name: Deep Neural Network Classification
D3A-DNNC.description: A deep neural network (DNN) is an artificial neural network (ANN) with multiple layers between the input and output layers.
D3A-DNNC.description_full: A deep neural network (DNN) is an artificial neural network (ANN) with multiple layers between the input and output layers. There are different types of neural networks but they always consist of the same components: neurons, synapses, weights, biases, and functions. These components as a whole function similarly to a human brain, and can be trained like any other ML algorithm

D3A-DP.name: Distribution Properties
D3A-DP.description: The properties derived from a probability distribution.
D3A-DP.description_full: The properties derived from a probability distribution.

D3A-DQ.name: Dyna-Q
D3A-DQ.description: A Dyna-Q agent combines acting, learning, and planning.
D3A-DQ.description_full: A Dyna-Q agent combines acting, learning, and planning.

D3A-DQL.name: Deep Q-learning
D3A-DQL.description: Uses a deep convolutional neural network, with layers of tiled convolutional filters to mimic the effects of receptive fields.
D3A-DQL.description_full: Uses a deep convolutional neural network, with layers of tiled convolutional filters to mimic the effects of receptive fields.

D3A-DR.name: Dimension Reduction
D3A-DR.description: Dimensionality reduction is a key technique within unsupervised learning.
D3A-DR.description_full: Dimensionality reduction is a key technique within unsupervised learning. It compresses the data by finding a smaller, different set of variables that capture what matters most in the original features, while minimizing the loss of information.

D3A-DS.name: Descriptive Statistics
D3A-DS.description: Descriptive statistics provide simple summaries about the sample and about the observations that have been made.
D3A-DS.description_full: Descriptive statistics provide simple summaries about the sample and about the observations that have been made.

D3A-DT.name: Decision Tree
D3A-DT.description: Decision tree learning is a supervised learning approach used in statistics, data mining, and machine learning.
D3A-DT.description_full: Decision tree learning is a supervised learning approach used in statistics, data mining, and machine learning. In this formalism, a classification or regression decision tree is used as a predictive model to draw conclusions about a set of observations.

D3A-DTR.name: Decision Tree Regression
D3A-DTR.description: Decision Trees Regression is asupervised learning method with the goal  to create a model that predicts the value of a target variable by learning simple decision rules inferred from the data features.
D3A-DTR.description_full: Decision Trees Regression is asupervised learning method with the goal  to create a model that predicts the value of a target variable by learning simple decision rules inferred from the data features

D3A-DWM.name: Density-weighted Method
D3A-DWM.description: An Actvie Learning technique that uses a density estimate meta-parameter to avoid sampling sparsely populated regions of the feature space and can be based parametrically or from a parameter free model.
D3A-DWM.description_full: An Actvie Learning technique that uses a density estimate meta-parameter to avoid sampling sparsely populated regions of the feature space and can be based parametrically or from a parameter free model.

D3A-EER.name: Expected Error Reduction
D3A-EER.description: Expected Error Reduction (EER) follows similar ideas as EMC, but again looks at the model output instead of the model itself and also takes the other data into account.
D3A-EER.description_full: Expected Error Reduction (EER) follows similar ideas as EMC, but again looks at the model output instead of the model itself and also takes the other data into account. In particular, a sample x is considered useful, if we can expect that knowing the label will reduce the future error on unseen samples

D3A-EL.name: Epistemic Logic
D3A-EL.description: Epistemic logic addresses modalities of knowledge; i.e., the certainty of sentences.
D3A-EL.description_full: Epistemic logic addresses modalities of knowledge; i.e., the certainty of sentences.

D3A-EM.name: Exact Matching
D3A-EM.description: Exact matching for numeric types is just the simple test for mathematical equivalence of the values being matched.
D3A-EM.description_full: Exact matching for numeric types is just the simple test for mathematical equivalence of the values being matched.

D3A-EMC.name: Expected Model Change
D3A-EMC.description: Supervised learning establishes a relationship between the known input and output variables to conduct a predictive analysis.
D3A-EMC.description_full: Supervised learning establishes a relationship between the known input and output variables to conduct a predictive analysis.

D3A-EST.name: Estimation
D3A-EST.description: Estimation represents ways or a process of learning and determining the population parameter based on the model fitted to the data.
D3A-EST.description_full: Estimation represents ways or a process of learning and determining the population parameter based on the model fitted to the data.

D3A-FL.name: Fuzzy Logic
D3A-FL.description: Fuzzy logic is a form of many-valued logic in which the truth value of variables may be any real number between 0 and 1.
D3A-FL.description_full: Fuzzy logic is a form of many-valued logic in which the truth value of variables may be any real number between 0 and 1.

D3A-FOL.name: First-order Logic
D3A-FOL.description: First-order logic is a collection of formal systems used in mathematics, philosophy, linguistics, and computer science.
D3A-FOL.description_full: First-order logic is a collection of formal systems used in mathematics, philosophy, linguistics, and computer science. First-order logic uses quantified variables over non-logical objects, and allows the use of sentences that contain variables.

D3A-GAKG.name: Goodman and Kruskal's Gamma
D3A-GAKG.description: Goodman-Kruskal $\\gamma$ is a measure of rank correlation between x and y and is given by $(n_c -n_d) / (n_c + n_d)$, where $n_c$ is the number of concordant pairs of the observations and $n_d$ is the number of discordant pairs.
D3A-GAKG.description_full: Goodman-Kruskal $\\gamma$ is a measure of rank correlation between x and y and is given by $(n_c -n_d) / (n_c + n_d)$, where $n_c$ is the number of concordant pairs of the observations and $n_d$ is the number of discordant pairs.

D3A-GAN.name: Generative Adversarial Network
D3A-GAN.description: Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) are an approach to generative modeling using deep learning methods, such as convolutional neural networks.
D3A-GAN.description_full: Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) are an approach to generative modeling using deep learning methods, such as convolutional neural networks.

D3A-GBC.name: Graph-based Clustering
D3A-GBC.description: Graph-based Clustering is a form of clustering where data is represented with graphs to identify clusters.
D3A-GBC.description_full: Graph-based Clustering is a form of clustering where data is represented with graphs to identify clusters.  We include Connection-based Clustering in this class.

D3A-GBDT.name: Gradient-Boosted Decision Tree
D3A-GBDT.description: A gradient-boosted decision tree is, as in other bagging and boosting methods, a method where the relatively 'weak' machine learning model (a decision tree) is used in an ensemble to form a 'strong' machine learning model.
D3A-GBDT.description_full: A gradient-boosted decision tree is, as in other bagging and boosting methods, a method where the relatively 'weak' machine learning model (a decision tree) is used in an ensemble to form a 'strong' machine learning model.

D3A-GBSSL.name: Graph-based Semi-supervised Learning
D3A-GBSSL.description: Graph-based Semi-Supervised Learning (GSSL) methods aim to classify unlabeled data by learning the graph structure and labeled data jointly.
D3A-GBSSL.description_full: Graph-based Semi-Supervised Learning (GSSL) methods aim to classify unlabeled data by learning the graph structure and labeled data jointly.

D3A-GC.name: Grid-CNN
D3A-GC.description: A class of neural networks that specializes in processing data that has a grid-like topology, such as an image.
D3A-GC.description_full: A class of neural networks that specializes in processing data that has a grid-like topology, such as an image.

D3A-GM.name: Geometric Mean
D3A-GM.description: The nth root of the product of the data values, where there are n of these.
D3A-GM.description_full: The nth root of the product of the data values, where there are n of these. This measure is valid only for data that are measured absolutely on a strictly positive scale.

D3A-GPT.name: GPT
D3A-GPT.description: Generative pre-trained transformers (GPT) are a type of large language model (LLM) and a prominent framework for generative artificial intelligence.
D3A-GPT.description_full: Generative pre-trained transformers (GPT) are a type of large language model (LLM) and a prominent framework for generative artificial intelligence.

D3A-GRU.name: Gated Recurrent Unit
D3A-GRU.description: The GRU is like a long short-term memory (LSTM) with a forget gate, but has fewer parameters than LSTM, as it lacks an output gate.
D3A-GRU.description_full: The GRU is like a long short-term memory (LSTM) with a forget gate, but has fewer parameters than LSTM, as it lacks an output gate. GRU's performance on certain tasks of polyphonic music modeling, speech signal modeling and natural language processing was found to be similar to that of LSTM

D3A-HAFBTL.name: Heterogeneous Asymmetric Feature-based Transfer Learning
D3A-HAFBTL.description: Asymmetric transformation mapping  transforms the source feature space to align with that of the target or the target to that of the source.
D3A-HAFBTL.description_full: Asymmetric transformation mapping  transforms the source feature space to align with that of the target or the target to that of the source. This, in effect, bridges the feature space gap and reduces the problem into a homogeneous transfer problem when further distribution differences need to be corrected.

D3A-HBTL.name: Hybrid-based Transfer Learning
D3A-HBTL.description: This method creates an asymmetric mapping from the target to the source and takes into account bias issues of cross-domain correspondences.
D3A-HBTL.description_full: This method creates an asymmetric mapping from the target to the source and takes into account bias issues of cross-domain correspondences.

D3A-HC.name: Hierarchical Clustering
D3A-HC.description: Hierarchical clustering (also called hierarchical cluster analysis or HCA) is a method of cluster analysis that seeks to build a hierarchy of clusters.
D3A-HC.description_full: Hierarchical clustering (also called hierarchical cluster analysis or HCA) is a method of cluster analysis that seeks to build a hierarchy of clusters.

D3A-HDC.name: High-dimension Clustering
D3A-HDC.description: The cluster analysis of data with anywhere from a few dozen to many thousands of dimensions.
D3A-HDC.description_full: The cluster analysis of data with anywhere from a few dozen to many thousands of dimensions.

D3A-HFBTL.name: Heterogeneous Feature-based Transfer Learning
D3A-HFBTL.description: Symmetric transformation  takes both the source feature space Xs and target feature space Xt and learns feature transformations as to project each onto a common subspace Xc for adaptation purposes.
D3A-HFBTL.description_full: Symmetric transformation  takes both the source feature space Xs and target feature space Xt and learns feature transformations as to project each onto a common subspace Xc for adaptation purposes. This derived subspace becomes a domain-invariant feature subspace to associate cross-domain data, and in effect, reduces marginal distribution differences.

D3A-HM.name: Harmonic Mean
D3A-HM.description: The reciprocal of the arithmetic mean of the reciprocals of the data values.
D3A-HM.description_full: The reciprocal of the arithmetic mean of the reciprocals of the data values. This measure too is valid only for data that are measured absolutely on a strictly positive scale.

D3A-HOL.name: Higher-order Logic
D3A-HOL.description: Higher-order logic is a form of predicate logic that is distinguished from first-order logic by additional quantifiers and, sometimes, stronger semantics.
D3A-HOL.description_full: Higher-order logic is a form of predicate logic that is distinguished from first-order logic by additional quantifiers and, sometimes, stronger semantics. Higher-order logics with their standard semantics are more expressive, but their model-theoretic properties are less well-behaved than those of first-order logic.

D3A-HT.name: Hypothesis Testing
D3A-HT.description: A statistical hypothesis test is a method of statistical inference used to decide whether the data at hand sufficiently support a particular hypothesis.
D3A-HT.description_full: A statistical hypothesis test is a method of statistical inference used to decide whether the data at hand sufficiently support a particular hypothesis. Hypothesis testing allows us to make probabilistic statements about population parameters.

D3A-HTL.name: Heterogeneous Transfer Learning
D3A-HTL.description: Heterogeneous transfer learning is characterized by the source and target domains having differing feature spaces, but may also be combined with other issues such as differing data distributions and label spaces.
D3A-HTL.description_full: Heterogeneous transfer learning is characterized by the source and target domains having differing feature spaces, but may also be combined with other issues such as differing data distributions and label spaces.

D3A-IBTL.name: Instance-based Transfer Learning
D3A-IBTL.description: Instance-based transfer learning methods try to reweight the samples in the source domain in an attempt to correct for marginal distribution differences.
D3A-IBTL.description_full: Instance-based transfer learning methods try to reweight the samples in the source domain in an attempt to correct for marginal distribution differences. These reweighted instances are then directly used in the target domain for training.

D3A-ID3.name: ID3
D3A-ID3.description: ID3 stands for Iterative Dichotomiser 3 and is named such because the algorithm iteratively (repeatedly) dichotomizes(divides) features into two or more groups at each step.
D3A-ID3.description_full: ID3 stands for Iterative Dichotomiser 3 and is named such because the algorithm iteratively (repeatedly) dichotomizes(divides) features into two or more groups at each step.

D3A-IE.name: Interval Estimation
D3A-IE.description: Interval estimation is the use of sample data to estimate an interval of possible values of a parameter of interest.
D3A-IE.description_full: Interval estimation is the use of sample data to estimate an interval of possible values of a parameter of interest.

D3A-IR.name: Interquartile Range
D3A-IR.description: The interquartile range (IQR) is a measure of statistical dispersion, which is the spread of the data and is defined as the difference between the 75th and 25th percentiles of the data.
D3A-IR.description_full: The interquartile range (IQR) is a measure of statistical dispersion, which is the spread of the data and is defined as the difference between the 75th and 25th percentiles of the data.

D3A-IS.name: Inferential Statistics
D3A-IS.description: Statistical inference is the process of using data analysis to infer properties of an underlying distribution of probability.
D3A-IS.description_full: Statistical inference is the process of using data analysis to infer properties of an underlying distribution of probability.

D3A-ISG.name: Image Synthesis GAN
D3A-ISG.description: Image synthesis thorugh the application of Generative Adversarial Networks.
D3A-ISG.description_full: Image synthesis thorugh the application of Generative Adversarial Networks.

D3A-ISSL.name: Intrinsically Semi-supervised Learning
D3A-ISSL.description: These methods directly optimize an objective function with components for labeled and unlabeled samples and do not rely on any intermediate steps or supervised base learners.
D3A-ISSL.description_full: These methods directly optimize an objective function with components for labeled and unlabeled samples and do not rely on any intermediate steps or supervised base learners. Basically, these methods are extension of existing supervised methods to include the effect of unlabeled data samples in the objective function.

D3A-ITITG.name: Image-to-Image Translation GAN
D3A-ITITG.description: Image-to-image translation is the task of transferring styles and characteristics from one image domain to another.
D3A-ITITG.description_full: Image-to-image translation is the task of transferring styles and characteristics from one image domain to another.

D3A-KCC.name: K-Center Clustering
D3A-KCC.description: K-center Clustering is a type of clustering based on an combinatorial optimization methods.
D3A-KCC.description_full: K-center Clustering is a type of clustering based on an combinatorial optimization methods.  It clusters a set of points so as to minimize the maximum intercluster distance.

D3A-KFCV.name: K-Fold Cross-Validation
D3A-KFCV.description: Cross-validation is a resampling procedure used to evaluate machine learning models on a limited data sample.
D3A-KFCV.description_full: Cross-validation is a resampling procedure used to evaluate machine learning models on a limited data sample. The procedure has a single parameter called k that refers to the number of groups that a given data sample is to be split into. As such, the procedure is often called k-fold cross-validation. When a specific value for k is chosen, it may be used in place of k in the reference to the model, such as k=10 becoming 10-fold cross-validation

D3A-KMC.name: K-means Clustering
D3A-KMC.description: K-means algorithm identifies k number of centroids, and then allocates every data point to the nearest cluster, while keeping the centroids as small as possible.
D3A-KMC.description_full: K-means algorithm identifies k number of centroids, and then allocates every data point to the nearest cluster, while keeping the centroids as small as possible.

D3A-KNN.name: K-Nearest Neighbors
D3A-KNN.description: The k-nearest neighbors algorithm, also known as KNN or k-NN, is a non-parametric, supervised learning classifier, which uses proximity to make classifications or predictions about the grouping of an individual data point.
D3A-KNN.description_full: The k-nearest neighbors algorithm, also known as KNN or k-NN, is a non-parametric, supervised learning classifier, which uses proximity to make classifications or predictions about the grouping of an individual data point.

D3A-KRCC.name: Kendall's Rank Correlation Coefficient
D3A-KRCC.description: Kendall's $\\tau$ between and is given by $(n_c - n_d) / \\sqrt((n_c+n_d+n_x)(n_c+n_d+n_y)$, where is the number of concordant pairs of observations, is the number of discordant pairs, is the number of ties involving only the variable, and is the number of ties involving only the variable." ;.
D3A-KRCC.description_full: Kendall's $\\tau$ between and is given by $(n_c - n_d) / \\sqrt((n_c+n_d+n_x)(n_c+n_d+n_y)$, where is the number of concordant pairs of observations, is the number of discordant pairs, is the number of ties involving only the variable, and is the number of ties involving only the variable." ;

D3A-KUR.name: Kurtosis
D3A-KUR.description: The measure of the "fatness" of the tails of a pmf or pdf.
D3A-KUR.description_full: The measure of the "fatness" of the tails of a pmf or pdf. The fourth standardized moment of the distribution.

D3A-LC.name: Linear Classifier
D3A-LC.description: A linear classifier is a model that makes a decision to categories a set of data points to a discrete class based on a linear combination of its explanatory variables.
D3A-LC.description_full: A linear classifier is a model that makes a decision to categories a set of data points to a discrete class based on a linear combination of its explanatory variables

D3A-LLP.name: Linear Logic Programming
D3A-LLP.description: Linear logic programming is a form of logic programming that uses linear logic, that is, it emphasizes the use of formulas as resources.
D3A-LLP.description_full: Linear logic programming is a form of logic programming that uses linear logic, that is, it emphasizes the use of formulas as resources.

D3A-LM.name: Levenschtein Matching
D3A-LM.description: The Levenshtein distance (LD) is a metric for measuring the differences between two sequences - or strings.
D3A-LM.description_full: The Levenshtein distance (LD) is a metric for measuring the differences between two sequences - or strings. Informally, the LD is the number of individual edits one would have to make to turn one sequence into another.

D3A-LP.name: Logic Programming
D3A-LP.description: Logic programming is a programming paradigm which is largely based on formal logic.
D3A-LP.description_full: Logic programming is a programming paradigm which is largely based on formal logic.

D3A-LR.name: Linear Regression
D3A-LR.description: Statistical regression method used for predictive analysis by modeling the linear relationship between independent and dependent variables.
D3A-LR.description_full: Statistical regression method used for predictive analysis by modeling the linear relationship between independent and dependent variables.

D3A-LRL.name: Logistic Regression Learning
D3A-LRL.description: A supervised learning method that builds a logistic regression model using training data.
D3A-LRL.description_full: A supervised learning method that builds a logistic regression model using training data.

D3A-LSTM.name: Long Short-term Memory
D3A-LSTM.description: Unlike standard feedforward neural networks, LSTM has feedback connections.
D3A-LSTM.description_full: Unlike standard feedforward neural networks, LSTM has feedback connections. Such a recurrent neural network (RNN) can process not only single data points (such as images), but also entire sequences of data (such as speech or video). This characteristic makes LSTM networks ideal for processing and predicting data

D3A-MA.name: Multivariate Analysis
D3A-MA.description: Multivariate statistics encompassed the simultaneous observation and analysis of more than one outcome variable, i.e., multivariate random variables.
D3A-MA.description_full: Multivariate statistics encompassed the simultaneous observation and analysis of more than one outcome variable, i.e., multivariate random variables.

D3A-MAD.name: Median Absolute Deviation
D3A-MAD.description: The median absolute deviation (also MAD) is the median of the absolute deviation from the median.
D3A-MAD.description_full: The median absolute deviation (also MAD) is the median of the absolute deviation from the median.

D3A-MAM.name: Moving Average Model
D3A-MAM.description: the moving-average model (MA model) is an approach for modeling univariate time series and specifies that the output variable is cross-correlated with a non-identical to itself random-variable.
D3A-MAM.description_full: the moving-average model (MA model) is an approach for modeling univariate time series and specifies that the output variable is cross-correlated with a non-identical to itself random-variable.

D3A-MBPO.name: Model-based Policy Optimization
D3A-MBPO.description: Model-based policy optimization (MBPO) is a model-based, online, off-policy reinforcement learning algorithm.
D3A-MBPO.description_full: Model-based policy optimization (MBPO) is a model-based, online, off-policy reinforcement learning algorithm. For more information on the different types of reinforcement learning agents

D3A-MBRL.name: Model-based Reinforcement Learning
D3A-MBRL.description: Model-based Reinforcement Learning refers to learning optimal behavior indirectly by learning a model of the environment by taking actions and observing the outcomes that include the next state and the immediate reward.
D3A-MBRL.description_full: Model-based Reinforcement Learning refers to learning optimal behavior indirectly by learning a model of the environment by taking actions and observing the outcomes that include the next state and the immediate reward. The models predict the outcomes of actions and are used in lieu of or in addition to interaction with the environment to learn optimal policies.

D3A-MBVI.name: Model-based Value Iteration
D3A-MBVI.description: Value Iteration effectively reducesthe evaluation stage down to a single sweep of the states.
D3A-MBVI.description_full: Value Iteration effectively reducesthe evaluation stage down to a single sweep of the states. Additionally, to improve things further, it combines the Policy Evaluation and Policy Improvement stages into a single update.

D3A-MEA.name: Mean
D3A-MEA.description: The sum of all measurements divided by the number of observations in the data set.
D3A-MEA.description_full: The sum of all measurements divided by the number of observations in the data set.

D3A-MED.name: Median
D3A-MED.description: The middle value that separates the higher half from the lower half of the data set.
D3A-MED.description_full: The middle value that separates the higher half from the lower half of the data set. The median and the mode are the only measures of central tendency that can be used for ordinal data, in which values are ranked relative to each other but are not measured absolutely.

D3A-MFRL.name: Model-free Reinforcement Learning
D3A-MFRL.description: In reinforcement learning (RL), a model-free algorithm (as opposed to a model-based one) is an algorithm which does not use the transition probability distribution (and the reward function) associated with the Markov decision process (MDP),which, in RL, represents the problem to be solved.
D3A-MFRL.description_full: In reinforcement learning (RL), a model-free algorithm (as opposed to a model-based one) is an algorithm which does not use the transition probability distribution (and the reward function) associated with the Markov decision process (MDP),which, in RL, represents the problem to be solved. The transition probability distribution (or transition model) and the reward function are often collectively called the "model" of the environment (or MDP), hence the name "model-free". A model-free RL algorithm can be thought of as an "explicit" trial-and-error algorithm. An example of a model-free algorithm is Q-learning.

D3A-ML.name: Machine Learning
D3A-ML.description: Machine learning techniques are computational methods that combine statistics, probability, and optimization to make accurate predictions and/or improve performance.
D3A-ML.description_full: Machine learning techniques are computational methods that combine statistics, probability, and optimization to make accurate predictions and/or improve performance.

D3A-MML.name: Maximum-margin Learning
D3A-MML.description: Maximum-margin classifiers attempt to maximize the distance between the given data points and the decision boundary.
D3A-MML.description_full: Maximum-margin classifiers attempt to maximize the distance between the given data points and the decision boundary

D3A-MOD.name: Mode
D3A-MOD.description: The most frequent value in the data set.
D3A-MOD.description_full: The most frequent value in the data set. This is the only central tendency measure that can be used with nominal data, which have purely qualitative category assignments.

D3A-MOM.name: Moments
D3A-MOM.description: With a probability distribution function, the the first moment is the expected value, the second central moment is the variance, the third standardized moment is the skewness, and the fourth standardized moment is the kurtosis.
D3A-MOM.description_full: With a probability distribution function, the the first moment is the expected value, the second central moment is the variance, the third standardized moment is the skewness, and the fourth standardized moment is the kurtosis.

D3A-MPC.name: Multilayer Perceptron Classification
D3A-MPC.description: A multilayer perceptron (MLP) is a fully connected class of feedforward artificial neural network (ANN).An MLP consists of at least three layers of nodes: an input layer, a hidden layer and an output layer.
D3A-MPC.description_full: A multilayer perceptron (MLP) is a fully connected class of feedforward artificial neural network (ANN).An MLP consists of at least three layers of nodes: an input layer, a hidden layer and an output layer.

D3A-MR.name: Multiple Regression
D3A-MR.description: Multiple (linear) regression attempts to model the relationship between two or more explanatory variables and a response variable by fitting a linear equation to observed data.
D3A-MR.description_full: Multiple (linear) regression attempts to model the relationship between two or more explanatory variables and a response variable by fitting a linear equation to observed data.

D3A-MRL.name: Multiple Regression Learning
D3A-MRL.description: A supervised learning method that builds a multiple regression model using training data.
D3A-MRL.description_full: A supervised learning method that builds a multiple regression model using training data.

D3A-NBC.name: Naive Bayes Classifier
D3A-NBC.description: The Naïve Bayes classifier is a supervised machine learning algorithm, which is used for classification tasks, like text classification.
D3A-NBC.description_full: The Naïve Bayes classifier is a supervised machine learning algorithm, which is used for classification tasks, like text classification. It is also part of a family of generative learning algorithms, meaning that it seeks to model the distribution of inputs of a given class or category.

D3A-NML.name: Non-monotonic Logic
D3A-NML.description: Non-monotonic logic is a formal logic whose conclusion relation is not monotonic.
D3A-NML.description_full: Non-monotonic logic is a formal logic whose conclusion relation is not monotonic. In other words, non-monotonic logics are devised to capture and represent defeasible inferences (cf. defeasible reasoning), i.e., a kind of inference in which reasoners draw tentative conclusions, enabling reasoners to retract their conclusion(s) based on further evidence.

D3A-NPM.name: Numeric Pattern Matching
D3A-NPM.description: Numeric pattern matching uses a pattern specification and sees if the numeric value matches that pattern--simple forms include exact matching and range matching.
D3A-NPM.description_full: Numeric pattern matching uses a pattern specification and sees if the numeric value matches that pattern--simple forms include exact matching and range matching.

D3A-NPT.name: Non-Parametric Tests
D3A-NPT.description: A non-parametric test relies is used when the underlying distribution of data is non-symmetric (non-normal distribution).
D3A-NPT.description_full: A non-parametric test relies is used when the underlying distribution of data is non-symmetric (non-normal distribution).

D3A-NR.name: Nonlinear Regression
D3A-NR.description: Nonlinear regression is a form of regression analysis in which observational data are modeled by a function which is a nonlinear combination of the model parameters and depends on one or more independent variables.
D3A-NR.description_full: Nonlinear regression is a form of regression analysis in which observational data are modeled by a function which is a nonlinear combination of the model parameters and depends on one or more independent variables.

D3A-NRL.name: Nonlinear Regression Learning
D3A-NRL.description: A supervised learning method that builds a non-linear regression model using training data.
D3A-NRL.description_full: A supervised learning method that builds a non-linear regression model using training data.

D3A-OWL.name: OWL
D3A-OWL.description: The Web Ontology Language (OWL) is a family of knowledge representation languages for authoring ontologies.
D3A-OWL.description_full: The Web Ontology Language (OWL) is a family of knowledge representation languages for authoring ontologies.

D3A-PBC.name: Projection-based Clustering
D3A-PBC.description: Projection Based Clustering is a clustering framework based on a chosen projection method and projection method a parameter-free high-dimensional data visualization technique.
D3A-PBC.description_full: Projection Based Clustering is a clustering framework based on a chosen projection method and projection method a parameter-free high-dimensional data visualization technique.

D3A-PBCC.name: Point-biserial Correlation Coefficient
D3A-PBCC.description: The point biserial correlation coefficient (rpb) is a correlation coefficient used when one variable (e.g.
D3A-PBCC.description_full: The point biserial correlation coefficient (rpb) is a correlation coefficient used when one variable (e.g. Y) is dichotomous; Y can either be "naturally" dichotomous, like whether a coin lands heads or tails, or an artificially dichotomized variable.

D3A-PBL.name: Perturbation-based Learning
D3A-PBL.description: Perturbation based methods are proposed under the smoothness assumption, which indicates that two data points close to each other in feature space are likely to have the same label.
D3A-PBL.description_full: Perturbation based methods are proposed under the smoothness assumption, which indicates that two data points close to each other in feature space are likely to have the same label.

D3A-PBTL.name: Parameter-based Transfer Learning
D3A-PBTL.description: The idea behind parameter-based methods is that a well-trained model on the source domain has learned a well-defined structure, and if two tasks are related, this structure can be transferred to the target model.
D3A-PBTL.description_full: The idea behind parameter-based methods is that a well-trained model on the source domain has learned a well-defined structure, and if two tasks are related, this structure can be transferred to the target model.

D3A-PC.name: Phi Coefficient
D3A-PC.description: The phi coefficient (or mean square contingency coefficient is a measure of association for two binary variables.
D3A-PC.description_full: The phi coefficient (or mean square contingency coefficient is a measure of association for two binary variables.

D3A-PCA.name: Principal Components Analysis
D3A-PCA.description: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is a statistic-based method of identifying patterns in a large dataset while increasing interpretability and preserving information.
D3A-PCA.description_full: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is a statistic-based method of identifying patterns in a large dataset while increasing interpretability and preserving information.

D3A-PE.name: Point Estimation
D3A-PE.description: A point estimation is a single value that estimates the parameter.
D3A-PE.description_full: A point estimation is a single value that estimates the parameter. Point estimates are single values calculated from the sample

D3A-PG.name: Policy Gradient
D3A-PG.description: The objective of a Reinforcement Learning Policy Gradient agent is to maximize the “expected” reward when following a policy.
D3A-PG.description_full: The objective of a Reinforcement Learning Policy Gradient agent is to maximize the “expected” reward when following a policy

D3A-PGG.name: Progressively Growing GAN
D3A-PGG.description: Progressive Growing GAN (ProGAN) is an extension to the GAN training process that allows for the stable training of generator models that can output large high-quality images.
D3A-PGG.description_full: Progressive Growing GAN (ProGAN) is an extension to the GAN training process that allows for the stable training of generator models that can output large high-quality images.

D3A-PIX.name: Pix2Pix
D3A-PIX.description: Pix2Pix is based on condtional GAN architecture and are trained on paired set of images or scenes from two domains to be used for translation.
D3A-PIX.description_full: Pix2Pix is based on condtional GAN architecture and are trained on paired set of images or scenes from two domains to be used for translation.

D3A-PL.name: Probabilistic Logic
D3A-PL.description: Probabilistic logic extends traditional logic truth tables with probabilistic expressions.
D3A-PL.description_full: Probabilistic logic extends traditional logic truth tables with probabilistic expressions.

D3A-PM.name: Pattern Matching
D3A-PM.description: Pattern matching is the act of checking a given sequence of tokens for the presence of the constituents of some pattern.
D3A-PM.description_full: Pattern matching is the act of checking a given sequence of tokens for the presence of the constituents of some pattern.

D3A-PRO.name: Prolog
D3A-PRO.description: Prolog has its roots in first-order logic, a formal logic, and unlike many other programming languages.
D3A-PRO.description_full: Prolog has its roots in first-order logic, a formal logic, and unlike many other programming languages.

D3A-PT.name: Parametric Tests
D3A-PT.description: A parametric test relies upon the assumption that the data you want to test is (or approximately is) normally distributed.
D3A-PT.description_full: A parametric test relies upon the assumption that the data you want to test is (or approximately is) normally distributed.

D3A-QBC.name: Query By Committee
D3A-QBC.description: Query by Committee (QBC) takes inspiration from ensemble methods.
D3A-QBC.description_full: Query by Committee (QBC) takes inspiration from ensemble methods. Instead of just one classifier, it takes into account the decision of a committee C=ℎ1,…,ℎc of classifiers ℎi. Each classifier has the same target classes, but a different underlying model or a different view on the data.

D3A-QL.name: Q-Learning
D3A-QL.description: Q-learning is a model-free reinforcement learning algorithm to learn the value of an action in a particular state.
D3A-QL.description_full: Q-learning is a model-free reinforcement learning algorithm to learn the value of an action in a particular state. It does not require a model of the environment (hence "model-free"), and it can handle problems with stochastic transitions and rewards without requiring adaptations.

D3A-RA.name: Regression Analysis
D3A-RA.description: Regression analysis is a set of statistical processes for estimating the relationships between a dependent variable (often called the 'outcome' or 'response' variable, or a 'label' in machine learning parlance) and one or more independent variables (often called 'predictors', 'covariates', 'explanatory variables' or 'features').
D3A-RA.description_full: Regression analysis is a set of statistical processes for estimating the relationships between a dependent variable (often called the 'outcome' or 'response' variable, or a 'label' in machine learning parlance) and one or more independent variables (often called 'predictors', 'covariates', 'explanatory variables' or 'features').

D3A-RAL.name: Regression Analysis Learning
D3A-RAL.description: Regression is used to understand the relationship between dependent and independent variables which is then used to make projections, such as for sales revenue for a given business.
D3A-RAL.description_full: Regression is used to understand the relationship between dependent and independent variables which is then used to make projections, such as for sales revenue for a given business.

D3A-RAN.name: Range
D3A-RAN.description: The range of a set of data is the difference between the largest and smallest value.
D3A-RAN.description_full: The range of a set of data is the difference between the largest and smallest value.

D3A-RBTL.name: Relational-based Transfer Learning
D3A-RBTL.description: Relational-based Transfer Learning is a subfield of machine learning where knowledge and patterns learned from one domain, characterized by relational and structured data, are transferred to enhance the learning of another related domain.
D3A-RBTL.description_full: Relational-based Transfer Learning is a subfield of machine learning where knowledge and patterns learned from one domain, characterized by relational and structured data, are transferred to enhance the learning of another related domain. This approach leverages shared concepts, relations, and structures across domains, taking advantage of the rich semantic knowledge within relational data to improve learning performance in the target task.

D3A-RC.name: Rank Correlation
D3A-RC.description: A rank correlation is any of several statistics that measure an ordinal association-the relationship between rankings of different ordinal variables or different rankings of the same variable, where a "ranking" is the assignment of the ordering labels "first", "second", "third", etc.
D3A-RC.description_full: A rank correlation is any of several statistics that measure an ordinal association-the relationship between rankings of different ordinal variables or different rankings of the same variable, where a "ranking" is the assignment of the ordering labels "first", "second", "third", etc. to different observations of a particular variable.

D3A-RE.name: Resampling Ensemble
D3A-RE.description: In the method, the small classes are oversampled and large classes are undersampled.
D3A-RE.description_full: In the method, the small classes are oversampled and large classes are undersampled. The resampling scale is determined by the ratio of the min class number and max class number. And multiple machine learning methods are selected to construct the ensemble

D3A-RF.name: Random Forest
D3A-RF.description: Random Forest is a ML method that combines several other ML methods.
D3A-RF.description_full: Random Forest is a ML method that combines several other ML methods. At its core, Random Forest is an ensemble method of multiple bootstrapped decision trees filled with training data and random feature selection.

D3A-RL.name: Reinforcement Learning
D3A-RL.description: Reinforcement Learning is a subjugate technique of machine learning that uses feedback to reinforce good or valid rules and lessen the reliance of bad or ineffective rules.
D3A-RL.description_full: Reinforcement Learning is a subjugate technique of machine learning that uses feedback to reinforce good or valid rules and lessen the reliance of bad or ineffective rules

D3A-RM.name: Regex Matching
D3A-RM.description: Regular expression matching is type of partial string matching using a regular expression, which is a sequence of characters that specifies a match pattern in text.
D3A-RM.description_full: Regular expression matching is type of partial string matching using a regular expression, which is a sequence of characters that specifies a match pattern in text.

D3A-RNN.name: Residual Neural Network
D3A-RNN.description: A residual neural network (ResNet) is an artificial neural network (ANN).
D3A-RNN.description_full: A residual neural network (ResNet) is an artificial neural network (ANN). It is a gateless or open-gated variant of the HighwayNet, the first working very deep feedforward neural network with hundreds of layers, much deeper than previous neural networks.

D3A-RS.name: Random Splits
D3A-RS.description: The dataset is repeatedly sampled with a random split of the data into train and test sets.
D3A-RS.description_full: The dataset is repeatedly sampled with a random split of the data into train and test sets.

D3A-SAR.name: SARSA
D3A-SAR.description: State-action-reward-state-action (SARSA) is an algorithm for learning a Markov decision process policy, used in the reinforcement learning area of machine learning.
D3A-SAR.description_full: State-action-reward-state-action (SARSA) is an algorithm for learning a Markov decision process policy, used in the reinforcement learning area of machine learning.

D3A-SC.name: Subspace Clustering
D3A-SC.description: Subspace clustering is an extension of traditional clustering that seeks to find clusters in different subspaces within a dataset.
D3A-SC.description_full: Subspace clustering is an extension of traditional clustering that seeks to find clusters in different subspaces within a dataset.

D3A-SD.name: Standard Deviation
D3A-SD.description: The standard deviation is a measure of the amount of variation or dispersion of a set of values.
D3A-SD.description_full: The standard deviation is a measure of the amount of variation or dispersion of a set of values.

D3A-SEM.name: String Equivalence Matching
D3A-SEM.description: String equivalence matching is a type of string pattern matching which is exact; that is, the strings being compared must have the same value for each character in their sequence and be of the same length.
D3A-SEM.description_full: String equivalence matching is a type of string pattern matching which is exact; that is, the strings being compared must have the same value for each character in their sequence and be of the same length.

D3A-SEQ.name: SeqGAN
D3A-SEQ.description: Sequence Generation Framework (SeqGAN) models the data generator as a stochastic policy in reinforcement learning (RL), SeqGAN bypasses the generator differentiation problem by directly performing gradient policy update.
D3A-SEQ.description_full: Sequence Generation Framework (SeqGAN) models the data generator as a stochastic policy in reinforcement learning (RL), SeqGAN bypasses the generator differentiation problem by directly performing gradient policy update.

D3A-SFTL.name: Symmetric Feature-based Transfer Learning
D3A-SFTL.description: Homogeneous symmetric transformation takes both the source feature space Xs and target feature space Xt and learns feature transformations as to project each onto a common subspace Xc for adaptation purposes.
D3A-SFTL.description_full: Homogeneous symmetric transformation takes both the source feature space Xs and target feature space Xt and learns feature transformations as to project each onto a common subspace Xc for adaptation purposes. This derived subspace becomes a domain-invariant feature subspace to associate cross-domain data, and in effect, reduces marginal distribution differences.

D3A-SKE.name: Skewness
D3A-SKE.description: Skewness is a measure of the asymmetry of the probability distribution of a real-valued random variable about its mean.
D3A-SKE.description_full: Skewness is a measure of the asymmetry of the probability distribution of a real-valued random variable about its mean. The standardized moment of a probability distribution function.

D3A-SL.name: Supervised Learning
D3A-SL.description: Supervised learning establishes a relationship between the known input and output variables to conduct a predictive analysis.
D3A-SL.description_full: Supervised learning establishes a relationship between the known input and output variables to conduct a predictive analysis.

D3A-SM.name: Statistical Method
D3A-SM.description: Building methods using the mathematical study of the likelihood and probability of events occurring based on known information and inferred by taking a limited number of samples.
D3A-SM.description_full: Building methods using the mathematical study of the likelihood and probability of events occurring based on known information and inferred by taking a limited number of samples.

D3A-SOM.name: Self-organizing Map
D3A-SOM.description: A Self-Organizing Map (SOM) is a unsupervised learning model in Artificial Neural Network where the feature maps are the generated two-dimensional discretized form of an input space during the model training (based on competitive learning).
D3A-SOM.description_full: A Self-Organizing Map (SOM) is a unsupervised learning model in Artificial Neural Network where the feature maps are the generated two-dimensional discretized form of an input space during the model training (based on competitive learning)

D3A-SPM.name: String Pattern Matching
D3A-SPM.description: String pattern-matching algorithms, also known as string-matching algorithms, are an important class of string algorithms that try to find a place where one or several strings (also called patterns) are found within a larger string or text.
D3A-SPM.description_full: String pattern-matching algorithms, also known as string-matching algorithms, are an important class of string algorithms that try to find a place where one or several strings (also called patterns) are found within a larger string or text

D3A-SR.name: Symbolic AI
D3A-SR.description: Symbolic artificial intelligence is the term for the collection of all methods in artificial intelligence that are based on high-level symbolic (human-readable) representations of problems, logic, and search.
D3A-SR.description_full: Symbolic artificial intelligence is the term for the collection of all methods in artificial intelligence that are based on high-level symbolic (human-readable) representations of problems, logic, and search.

D3A-SSB.name: Semi-supervised Boosting
D3A-SSB.description: Boosting methods can be readily extended to the semi-supervised setting, by introducing pseudo-labeled data after each learning step; which gives rise to the idea of semi-supervised boosting methods.
D3A-SSB.description_full: Boosting methods can be readily extended to the semi-supervised setting, by introducing pseudo-labeled data after each learning step; which gives rise to the idea of semi-supervised boosting methods. The pseudo-labeling approach of self- training and co-training can be easily extended to boosting methods. Several boosting methods such as SSMBoost, ASSEMBLE, SemiBoost, RegBoost, etc can be found which can be applied for utilizing unlabeled datasets for supervised classifiers.

D3A-SSCT.name: Semi-supervised Co-training
D3A-SSCT.description: Multi-view co-training involves training the classifiers in completely different views of training data.
D3A-SSCT.description_full: Multi-view co-training involves training the classifiers in completely different views of training data. On the other hand, single-view co-training methods are generally applied as ensemble methods.

D3A-SSCTL.name: Semi-supervised Cluster-then-label
D3A-SSCTL.description: Pre-training methods are aimed to guide the parameters of a network towards interesting regions in model space using unlabeled data, before fine-tuning the parameters with the labeled data.
D3A-SSCTL.description_full: Pre-training methods are aimed to guide the parameters of a network towards interesting regions in model space using unlabeled data, before fine-tuning the parameters with the labeled data.

D3A-SSFE.name: Semi-supervised Feature Extraction
D3A-SSFE.description: Feature extraction refers to reducing the number of dimensions in a data point so that it is computationally feasible and effective to learn a model.
D3A-SSFE.description_full: Feature extraction refers to reducing the number of dimensions in a data point so that it is computationally feasible and effective to learn a model.

D3A-SSGML.name: Semi-supervised Generative Model Learning
D3A-SSGML.description: A Semi Supervised Machine Learning model which assume that the distributions take some particular form p(x|y,theta) parameterized by the vector.
D3A-SSGML.description_full: A Semi Supervised Machine Learning model which assume that the distributions take some particular form p(x|y,theta) parameterized by the vector. If these assumptions are incorrect, the unlabeled data may actually decrease the accuracy of the solution relative to what would have been obtained from labeled data alone. However, if the assumptions are correct, then the unlabeled data necessarily improves performance.

D3A-SSIL.name: Semi-supervised Inductive Learning
D3A-SSIL.description: The goal of inductive learning is to infer the correct mapping from X to Y.
D3A-SSIL.description_full: The goal of inductive learning is to infer the correct mapping from X to Y.

D3A-SSL.name: Semi-Supervised Learning
D3A-SSL.description: Semi-supervised learning is a branch of machine learning that combines a small amount of labeled data with a large amount of unlabeled data during training.
D3A-SSL.description_full: Semi-supervised learning is a branch of machine learning that combines a small amount of labeled data with a large amount of unlabeled data during training.

D3A-SSML.name: Semi-supervised Manifold Learning
D3A-SSML.description: A version of Semi-Supervised Learning that applies the Manifold assumption that the data like approximately on a manifold of much lower dimension than the input space.
D3A-SSML.description_full: A version of Semi-Supervised Learning that applies the Manifold assumption that the data like approximately on a manifold of much lower dimension than the input space.

D3A-SSPT.name: Semi-supervised Pre-training
D3A-SSPT.description: Pre-training methods are aimed to guide the parameters of a network towards interesting regions in model space using unlabeled data, before fine-tuning the parameters with the labeled data.
D3A-SSPT.description_full: Pre-training methods are aimed to guide the parameters of a network towards interesting regions in model space using unlabeled data, before fine-tuning the parameters with the labeled data

D3A-SSST.name: Semi-supervised Self-training
D3A-SSST.description: Self-training is the procedure in which a supervised method for classification or regression is modified it to work in a semi-supervised manner, taking advantage of labeled and unlabeled data.
D3A-SSST.description_full: Self-training is the procedure in which a supervised method for classification or regression is modified it to work in a semi-supervised manner, taking advantage of labeled and unlabeled data

D3A-SSTL.name: Semi-supervised Transductive Learning
D3A-SSTL.description:
The goal of transductive learning is to infer the correct labels for the given unlabeled data
x_{l+1},..
D3A-SSTL.description_full:
The goal of transductive learning is to infer the correct labels for the given unlabeled data
x_{l+1},... ,x_{l+u} only

D3A-SSWM.name: Semi-supervised Wrapper Method
D3A-SSWM.description: The principle behind wrapper methods is that we train a model with labeled data and then generate pseudo-labels for the unlabeled data using the trained model iteratively.
D3A-SSWM.description_full: The principle behind wrapper methods is that we train a model with labeled data and then generate pseudo-labels for the unlabeled data using the trained model iteratively.

D3A-STA.name: Stacking
D3A-STA.description: Stacking is a method of using the results and predictions from one layer of ML models as inputs to another layer of ML models.
D3A-STA.description_full: Stacking is a method of using the results and predictions from one layer of ML models as inputs to another layer of ML models. Stacking (sometimes called stacked generalization) involves training a model to combine the predictions of several other learning algorithms.

D3A-SVD.name: Singular Value Decomposition
D3A-SVD.description: Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) is an algorithm that represents a matrix as a linear series of data and to find the set of factors that will best predict an outcome.
D3A-SVD.description_full: Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) is an algorithm that represents a matrix as a linear series of data and to find the set of factors that will best predict an outcome

D3A-SVMC.name: Support Vector Machine Classification
D3A-SVMC.description: Support Vector Machine (SVM) is a robust classification and regression technique that maximizes the predictive accuracy of a model without overfitting the training data.
D3A-SVMC.description_full: Support Vector Machine (SVM) is a robust classification and regression technique that maximizes the predictive accuracy of a model without overfitting the training data. SVM is particularly suited to analyzing data with very large numbers (for example, thousands) of predictor fields.

D3A-TBL.name: Transformer-based Learning
D3A-TBL.description: A transformer is a deep learning model.
D3A-TBL.description_full: A transformer is a deep learning model. It is distinguished by its adoption of self-attention, differentially weighting the significance of each part of the input (which includes the recursive output) data.

D3A-TDL.name: Temporal Difference Learning
D3A-TDL.description: Temporal difference (TD) learning refers to a class of model-free reinforcement learning methods which learn by bootstrapping from the current estimate of the value function.
D3A-TDL.description_full: Temporal difference (TD) learning refers to a class of model-free reinforcement learning methods which learn by bootstrapping from the current estimate of the value function. These methods sample from the environment, like Monte Carlo methods, and perform updates based on current estimates, like dynamic programming methods

D3A-TL.name: Temporal Logic
D3A-TL.description: Temporal logic addresses the semantics of tense; i.e., qualifying expressions of when.
D3A-TL.description_full: Temporal logic addresses the semantics of tense; i.e., qualifying expressions of when.

D3A-TM.name: Trimmed Mean
D3A-TM.description: The arithmetic mean of data values after a certain number or proportion of the highest and lowest data values have been discarded.
D3A-TM.description_full: The arithmetic mean of data values after a certain number or proportion of the highest and lowest data values have been discarded.

D3A-TSA.name: Time Series Analysis
D3A-TSA.description: Time series analysis comprises methods for analyzing time series data in order to extract meaningful statistics and other characteristics of the data.
D3A-TSA.description_full: Time series analysis comprises methods for analyzing time series data in order to extract meaningful statistics and other characteristics of the data.

D3A-TSC.name: t-SNE Clustering
D3A-TSC.description: T-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) is a statistical method for visualizing high-dimensional data by giving each datapoint a location in a two or three-dimensional map.
D3A-TSC.description_full: T-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) is a statistical method for visualizing high-dimensional data by giving each datapoint a location in a two or three-dimensional map.

D3A-TX.name: Transformer-XL
D3A-TX.description: Transformer-XL is a transformer architecture that introduces the notion of recurrence to the deep self-attention network.
D3A-TX.description_full: Transformer-XL is a transformer architecture that introduces the notion of recurrence to the deep self-attention network. Instead of computing the hidden states from scratch for each new segment, Transformer-XL reuses the hidden states obtained in previous segments.

D3A-UL.name: Unsupervised Learning
D3A-UL.description: Unsupervised learning creates relationships with unlabeled data without the input of a human or other outside actor.
D3A-UL.description_full: Unsupervised learning creates relationships with unlabeled data without the input of a human or other outside actor. Uses only input data.

D3A-UP.name: Unsupervised Preprocessing
D3A-UP.description: When performing unsupervised learning, the machine is presented with unlabeled data.
D3A-UP.description_full: When performing unsupervised learning, the machine is presented with unlabeled data. (Unlabeled data has no target.) Unsupervised learning algorithms seek to discover intrinsic patterns that underlie the data, such as a clustering parameter or a redundant parameter (dimension) that can be reduced.

D3A-US.name: Uncertainty Sampling
D3A-US.description: Makes the utility inversely proportional to the uncertainty of the model with respect to the sample and will work with any  model provided it can assess its uncertainty of a predection.
D3A-US.description_full: Makes the utility inversely proportional to the uncertainty of the model with respect to the sample and will work with any  model provided it can assess its uncertainty of a predection.

D3A-VAR.name: Variance
D3A-VAR.description: Variance is a measure of dispersion, meaning it is a measure of how far a set of numbers is spread out from their average value.
D3A-VAR.description_full: Variance is a measure of dispersion, meaning it is a measure of how far a set of numbers is spread out from their average value.

D3A-VOT.name: Voting
D3A-VOT.description: Voting is another form of ensembling.
D3A-VOT.description_full: Voting is another form of ensembling.

D3A-VR.name: Variance Reduction
D3A-VR.description: Leverages a well-known result from statistical learning and decomposes the model error into a data noise term, a model bias term and a model variance term.
D3A-VR.description_full: Leverages a well-known result from statistical learning and decomposes the model error into a data noise term, a model bias term and a model variance term. As the noise term only depends on the data and the bias is induced by the choice of model, any reduction in the error can only come from the variance term.

D3A-WM.name: Weighted Mean
D3A-WM.description: A mean that incorporates weighting to certain data elements.
D3A-WM.description_full: A mean that incorporates weighting to certain data elements.

D3F-UGPH.name: User Group Permissions
D3F-UGPH.description: Access control where access is determined based on attributes associated with users and the objects being accessed.
D3F-UGPH.description_full: Access control where access is determined based on attributes associated with users and the objects being accessed.
